items.appearanceHtml:
Main Level

Level 0 resembles an office from around the 1970s with a bright yellow wallpaper and a damp, unkept carpet. The level is quite average sized and has two layers.

The Smaller Area

if you are entering this level from Level 8, you will find yourself in a separate and much smaller area of the level. There is a room in this area as well known as The Manilla Room, and there are boards that block off Level 0.11 in The Manilla Room. The Hub Door can also be found in this area as well. There is also a small part of Level 0.2 you can find close by. To get back to the rest of Level 0 from this area, in The Manilla Room, you must have a Crowbar to break a Vent. This Vent will bring you back to the rest of the level.

extraSections.title: Update History

extraSections.html: This level was one that was added on the game's Release. The level had the same layout, just with checkerboard Pitfalls, and there was also no second layer. The Ladder room had the Ladder already fixed, and the exit could have spawned in either the broken ladder room, or on the other side of the Pitfalls which displayed the Ladder instead of a vent. All you would have to do is touch the Ladder (even though the box led up to nothing) and then you could move onto Level 1.
extraSections.title: Release Version

extraSections.html:
• In the second exit after you fall down in the Pitfalls, there is a Flashlight on a table in the dark room beside the staircase. This is one of the only Items you can find in Level 0, with the other Item being the Rope.
  • The Pitfalls were originally completely different, and instead of narrow pathways, they were in a checkerboard pattern. If you fell, you would get sent to a completely random area of the Level.
  • Level 0 has the most amount of Sublevels related to it out of any other level in the entire game, and the only one to have more than one.
  • In order to model the level, Fancy just copied and pasted a bunch of cubes around and added textures. He also mentioned how making the Backrooms is really easy because of this.
  • This level was originally going to be the same room over and over again infinitely. This was the original procedural generation system.
  • The original procedural generation system was implemented because of Level 0's size and so Fancy didn't have to copy and paste the Level for hours.
  • In the original Backrooms lore, Level 0 is completely devoid of entities. However, Fancy added the Bacteria to it to add some gameplay.
  • Before the game was released, the walls had another different texture that looked much different. Fancy mentioned that he changed the texture in his third Devlog.
  • In Fancy's 9th Devlog, he showed this level with Noclipping Floors and mentioned how he added them to a few levels, which might be hinting at Noclipping Floors coming to this level and others in the future.
  • There is another promotional image of the level, which is assumed to be the main level picture but at a different angle.
  • There was once a bug in the Pitfalls where players can walk over the entire Pitfalls in milliseconds, this was used by speedrunners.
  • There's a bug where you can't fall into the Pitfalls. To do this, you need to go to the edge of the Pitfalls and then look at the walls.

items.walkthroughHtml:
New game

This is the objective for starting a new game.

Part 1: Fixing the Ladder

Upon starting a new game, a cutscene plays where the player is with another Wanderer in a hallway. The Wanderer asks the player, "Mind uh, checking for anyone's still vacuuming that backroom?", and the player enters the backroom. After walking for a short while, they find that the door they entered through is gone, and the game begins. Upon spawning in, you will either find yourself in a room with a bunch of pillars, or an area that's right next to the pillar room. Both of these spawn points have a Note on a wall nearby. You need to look around the level until you find a room with a broken Ladder. Collect all 4 of the ladder pieces to fix it, and then a cutscene will play of the player climbing up the Ladder to get to a small box at the top and obtain a Key. Once the Key is obtained, head left of the Ladder until you find a wooden door. Unlock the door to enter the Pitfalls.

Part 2: The Pitfalls

The locked door leads to a room that is different compared to the rest of the level. It contains a set of walkways and pits called the Pitfalls, and on the other side of the room is the exit. You must balance yourself across them using Q and E and make it to the other side. On the other side, there is some Rope (that can be used to retrieve anyone else who has fallen down) and a Vent that leads to Level 1. Failing to balance yourself will lead to you falling in the holes, leading to the second layer. The second layer is similar to the rest of Level 0, though being much darker and having much more open space. An alternate exit can be found by following arrows drawn on the wall. After following the arrows for some time, you will find a room that looks completely different from the rest of the level. It looks to be a normal-ish house, featuring a kitchen that has blue lights, a completely dark room that only has a drawer with a Flashlight on top, and stairs that lead to a hallway with a Vent. Entering the Vent will send you to Level 1.

From Level 8

This is the objective for entering from Level 8.

Part 1: Figuring out the Locker Code

Once you enter the level from Level 8, you will be in the smaller area. In the miniature Level 0.2, there is a Light Switch on the wall. Use it and the lights will go out for a short time. when this happens, over a number on the wall, a shape will appear over a certain one. This is the code for a Locker in The Manilla Room. The only difference about this code is of course, it uses shapes. The numbers represent the order you put in the code, and the shapes represent what that button will be. Every single time you press the light switch, a shape will appear over the next number.

Part 2: Entering Level 0.11

If you enter the Locker code correctly, the Locker will open, and there will be a Chainsaw inside. If you forget or need the code for any other reason it's Square, Circle, Arrow. Use this Chainsaw to break down the boards that lead to Level 0.11, and once done, enter the small Level 0.11 room on the other side to proceed on. When in the entrance to the Level for a short time, you will noclip through the ground and find yourself in the Level.

items.summary: Level 1 is a large, sprawling warehouse that features concrete floors and walls, exposed rebar, and a low-hanging fog with no discernable source.
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Unlock the Elevator

Once you first spawn in, you will find an Elevator with a Color-Coded Lock that will send you to floor 2, but you need a code to access it. You will need to explore the level to find a red door that leads to a room with 4 different colored cars. The colors of the cars are the code, so remember the colors so you can enter the code in the elevator to enter the next area. For example, if 1 car is blue, 2 cars are red and 1 car is green, the code is 1 blue button, 2 red buttons and 1 green button. Inside the office rooms around the level, you can find useful Items such as Almond Water and Flashlights. Use Almond Water to restore your Sanity, because it will begin to drain in this floor.

Part 2: Get the Keys

Floor 2 is just like the first one, but much bigger. In this floor, you need to explore the rooms in order to find 4 Keys. The Keys can be found on desks, closets, and open drawers. You can also find Almond Water and Flashlights in this floor just like the first one. There is also a Skin-Stealer in this floor (2 if you are playing in Multiplayer with 3 or more Players), so be careful. After you find all 4 of the Keys, look around for a room that has a vault door. The vault door leads to floor 3, however it needs the Keys to open. Insert the 4 Keys into 4 different Keyholes that are around the room and have them all turned at the same time. They will unturn over time, so you have to be quick. If they're all turned at the same time, the vault door will open, allowing you to move onto floor 3.

Part 3: Find the Elevator

Floor 3 is a dimly-lit-area that has 3 individual floors. All of the floors are the exact same thing, except each one has a slight layout change. At the start, you'll be in a room made out of bricks, and it has an opening to stairs. Go down the stairs, and you'll find a more normal looking room with shelves containing Flashlights and Almond Water. Open the door, and you'll find the main part of the Level. To move on, look around for an Elevator that will bring you to floor 4. There is one possible Elevator spawn location for each floor, and the floor the Elevator is on is random. If you find a locked door, that means that's the door the Elevator can spawn on in the floor you're on, but it didn't. You're not alone in this floor however, because there is a Skin-Stealer roaming around. There's also only Items at the start of the floor, so make sure to use them wisely.

Part 4: Unlock the Gate

Floor 4 is a small room with much rougher concrete walls, and a closed gate. You must get through this gate in order to escape, but security override is needed to flip the switch to open the gate. In order to get access to flip the switch, you will need to go to The Hub. The Hub Door will be on the left side of the room and should be fairly simple to find. Once you enter, use the Computers in The M.E.G. Base to get access to the switch and then return to the Level.

Part 5: STAY iN THE LiGHT

Once the gate is open, you'll find an area resembling a parking garage. There are a few small hallways which the Player will have to run toward to survive all the Smilers when the lights turn off, and they contain two blue Double Doors which can either lead to a small empty room, a small room with a few Office Drawers (which can contain Flashlights and Almond Water), or the entrance to Level 2 (which will be random each time). After the lights switch back on, it will be safe to proceed to the next hallway. It's recommended you only go to one hallway at a time, because the lights only stay on for 5-10 seconds, and very rarely stay on for up to 20 seconds. There is also a ramp leading to a second floor in this floor, but it's highly recommended not to go to unless you've discovered the Wrong Door Ending, because that would allow you to use an ID Card on a M.E.G. sealed door to get to Level 9223372036854775807.

extraSections.title: Unlocking Doors

extraSections.html:
Level 0

To find Level 0's Door, you must enter the Level from Level 8 to get to the smaller area because that's where it is. Start in The Manilla Room, and go forward/away from it, then head a bit right before until you can’t go more forward. Look around the place, and you should be able to find the Door.

Level 1

To find Level 1's Door, you must be in floor 4. The Door will be out in the open, and clearly noticeable. You also need to unlock this Door to progress.

Level 4

To find Level 4's Door, after getting down the staircase at the start of the Level, go to the room at the top, and you will enter a room with nothing in it. Go through the next Door, and you will find a large Pillar Room. Go to the left of the room, and on the left side, the Second Door is The Hub Door.

Level 37

To find Level 37's Door, enter the square entrances, and enter the room at the top. You will find this large room with a staircase. Go up the staircase, and at the top is the Door.

Level 94

To find Level 94's Door, head to the very first house in the Level, and go upstairs. Open the door, and in the room is the Door.

Level 7

To find Level 7's Door, you must go the upstairs area and go through the door at the top of the room. Go through the door, then go up the stairs and at the top is the Door.

Level 9

To find Level 9's Door, you must find a specific house within the Level and enter it. Roam around the house until you find a door downstairs that leads to a stone staircase and at the bottom of it is the Door. Keep in mind that there is a Wretch guarding the upstairs area of the house.

Level 3999

To find Level 3999's Door, go through the sliding doors at the start of the Level and there will be a staff only door on the left. Behind it is The Hub.

Level 188

To find Level 188's Door, go forward until you find a room with a bunch of doors with labels of different Levels. Go through the door labelled "H" and go through the Hallway. At the end of it is the Door.

Level 52

To find Level 52's Door, in the starting room, go to the right side of the room. On the right side of the room is the Door.

Once you arrive from Level 1, you must go to The M.E.G. Base and enter it. Unlock the entrance to the Base and enter it. Unlock a Computer by putting in the password "itheardyou" and open the file "Gate.exe" to unlock the gate.

The wall text (and the wall itself) at the start was also gone because you had to go right. The Flashlight was automatic and would turn on the moment you entered the dark area, and the music in the Level Fun entrance was the old Row Row Row Your Boat instead of the You Day! Instrumental.

extraSections.html:
• This level is one of the few levels which has multiple main path entrances & exits, as well as the first level in the game to have multiple.
  • The ambience of this level is not made by Fancy or ins0mic but instead made by Baceero 2. You can find the original ambience that was used here.
  • If you enter Level 2 for the first time and go right, using cheats to get past the Smiler and reach the Level Fun entrance, prior to Version 1.0, it wasn't possible to enter Level Fun from the entrance, but after Version 1.0 it became possible.
  • Prior to Version 1.0, Gas was not deadly.
  • Fancy added Lighthead to the level to make it not as safe as walking forward but was soon replaced by the full body Smiler.
  • During the development of this level, prior to the addition of the entrance to Level Fun, the exit was a white light, likely just being a placeholder.
  • Level 2 in the original Backrooms lore goes by the name "Abandoned Utility Halls" on the Backrooms Wikidot and still has the name "Pipe Dreams" on the Backrooms Fandom Wiki.

items.summary: Level 2 consists mainly of dark, grey, concrete maintenance tunnels, stretching on for miles with walls lined with pipes.
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: The Station

Once you first spawn in, you will spot a wall that points left. Going left leads to the Station, and going right leads to Fun. If you are entering this level for the first time, you will have to go left first. If you try to go right first, a Smiler will stop you from doing so and will be blocking the path until you complete the left path. After traveling for a bit, there will be a dark area with a Flashlight on a small shelf on the wall. Pick up this flashlight so you can see in the dark. Halfway down the hallway, two pipes will burst. Walking through the Steam will kill you in a similar way to the hydrolitis plague in level 37.2. Once you are almost in the light, a Smiler will start chasing you down, forcing you to run. When the Smiler enters areas with light, the lights will break, so if you hear lights breaking, it may already be too late. Eventually, you will find a turnoff for the entrance to the Station. Keep an eye out for it, because it is very easy to miss if you aren't paying attention. It will act as a safe zone from the Smiler, so once you enter the entrance, you no longer have to worry about the Smiler. If you pass the entrance, you will eventually find yourself in a dead end with nothing you can do.

Part 2: FUN =)

After escaping the boiler room in Level 5, you will find yourself at a different entrance to level 2 on the opposite side. The wall in front points left with the message "Fun!" Once you arrive at the darkness again, get the Flashlight and enter the darkness. The only thing that makes the right path different from the left one is that only one pipe will burst. The Smiler will chase you again once almost out of the darkness. Instead of finding an entrance to the station, you will find an entrance to Level Fun. There will also be flashing colorful lights, so you know this is the exit as well as an instrumental edition of You Day! playing. Touch a ladder and you will end up in Level Fun. Just like last time, if you pass the exit this time, you will once again find a dead end.

items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: East Substation

Once you first spawn in, you will find yourself in a very long hallway, and at the end are locked bars. These bars lead to Level 4, so you will need to unlock them in order to escape. There is a box next to the locked bars containing a list of Fuse Boxes that must be turned on. The entrance door to the rest of the level will be locked at first but will open when you get near the box. You will then find yourself in the first zone, the east substation. In this zone, you must go around and look for Fuse Boxes. Enable all 3 of the Fuse Boxes in this zone, and more locked bars at the entrance of zone 1 will open allowing you to move to the second zone.

The Normal Fuse Box

All you have to do is flip all of the switches, and it will be enabled.

Part 2: West Substation

The closed bars at the start of zone 1 lead to this big room with red and white bricks, and 2 more closed-off bars, however the one at the start will be open. These bars lead to zone 2, the west substation. zone 2 is much darker and dangerous than the first zone, and once again you must find 3 Fuse Boxes and turn them on. After travelling for a bit, you will come across a hallway. But before you get the chance to open the door in this hall, a Hound will come up and start chasing you, so make sure you have a Flashlight out to blind it. There are only 2 Hounds in this zone, so it's not that hard.

Part 3: Generator

Zone 3 known as Generator is the most dangerous zone of them all. After turning on all 3 of the Fuse Boxes in zone 2, the other bars in the big room will be open. The third zone is very dark, so make sure you have a Flashlight out to see. The first room in zone 3 will already have a Hound. Hounds aren't the only danger in this zone, because a new hazard is introduced which are simply puddles with electricity in them. All you have to do to avoid them is to just not step in them. Simple as that. What makes the zone even harder is that the Fuse Boxes are now puzzles. There are only 2 kinds of puzzles though, which include:

Triway

This puzzle involves a bunch of electricity connectors which have 3 fuses on each one and all of the connectors form a hexagon shape. Every one of the fuse colors are different, but there's only 3 colors they can be which include green, blue, and red. You can turn the connectors and if 2 of the same fuse colors are aiming at each other, electricity will form. The goal is to get them all to match up.

Colored Wires

This puzzle is much simpler and easier than Triway. On the left side, there will be a bunch of wires, and they are each labelled with different colors. The colors include green, blue, red, and yellow. On the right side lies what the wires must be connected to. Connect the correct colors with each one and that's it.

Part 4: All of the Fuse Boxes Finally on

After turning on all of the Fuse Boxes in zone 3, the bars from the start of the level will be open, and you will be able to get to Level 4.

Some rooms on this level has windows, though most of them have been completely blacked out. Any windows that have not been blacked out are considered traps and should be avoided at all costs.
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: The Vending Machines

Once you first spawn in, look around for a room with 4 different vending machines. The hallway leading to it has 2 Window entities on the ends, so don't go up to them. There will be a door in this room that will let you proceed on, but it's locked. In order to unlock it, you have to use the vending machines in the room. Every vending machine is associated with an object. The objects Include chairs, tables, water coolers, and stacks of books. Count how many of that object is in the room, and on the vending machines, instead of buttons for a certain object, there are buttons that display numbers. Select the number of objects on each one and if all the values are right, the door should open.

Part 2: Sneak past the Cameras

After the vending machine puzzle, you will find yourself in a room full of cubicles. Here, you must find the room (which is located at the very end of the room) that leads to this hallway with a wall that has a button on it. The button says "Reset Cameras". Press this Button, and then you must sneak past 4 different Cameras scattered throughout a bunch of rooms. Sneaking past the Cameras is impossible without being spotted, so you must take shortcuts in order to get past them. If you get spotted by one, the Camera with flash red and an alarm will start playing. You'll eventually arrive at this brick hallway with a security office connected to it that has Crowbars on the desk and monitors that monitor the cameras. These monitors will say "SPOTTED!" If you were noticed by a Camera. If one of them says "SPOTTED!", the door that will take you to Level 5 will be locked, and you will have to take a Crowbar and break open the Vent in the hallway to get back to the beginning to try again. Don't get seen by any Cameras, and the door should open. It will take you to Level 5.

extraSections.html:
The Main Hall

The Main Hall is the main floor of Level 5 and the spawning location of the player when they first enter Level 5. These halls branch off into smaller rooms. The Main Hall has several pieces of furniture with a 30s era design. There are chandeliers on the ceiling, some lamps, couches, pillars, chairs, and plants on the side.

The Main Hall has differentiating rooms with one being a café, another room being The Beverly Room, and another room that looks like a completely different hotel. There are many branching areas, and also two bathrooms, a staircase going to an upper area, and a Lobby. The Lobby has a few seating areas and a check in. Only Male Deathmoths spawn in The Main Hall.

The Hotel

The Hotel Floor is simply a hotel. It has carpeted flooring and a bunch of numbered rooms. It also has 3 smaller rooms that contain Items. The spawn room has a bar and some Mailboxes. Almond Water, Energy Bars, and Juice can be found everywhere in The Hotel. The lights may be off in some places, making those areas dark. The majority of the hotel rooms have a Bed, a dresser, a mirror, a Closet, a nightstand, and a connecting bathroom with a bathtub and toilet.

The Boiler Room

The Boiler Room is very different compared to The Main Hall and The Hotel. It has pipes running along the walls similar to Level 2 and has stone walls and floors. The floor is also the main home of Female Deathmoths. There are occasionally pipes with Valves that spew large amounts of Bug Spray to scare off the Deathmoths. Occasionally, hotter areas and boilers can be found scattered throughout the Boiler Room.

If you hide under the Bed or in the Closet, the Skin-Stealer will be stuck on the spot and loops the sound cue of a Skin-Stealer chasing. If you get out, the Skin-Stealer would not attack unless you try to move, where the jumpscare will be played.

items.summary: Level 5 is a hotel complex, with many rooms and halls. This level itself seems to have been constructed in the 1930s, with furniture dating back to 1920.
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: The Main Hall

The Paintings

Once you first spawn in, you will find yourself in the Lobby of The Main Hall. There are four paintings on the walls and a check-in desk with 4 cans of Bug Spray. Make sure to grab some, you'll need it for later. Each one of the paintings is a person of a different age: A young girl, a young adult man, a middle-aged man, and an old man. Under each painting is a button. Press the buttons in order from the youngest person in the painting to the oldest person. Once you do that, the door to the rest of The Main Hall will open.

The Male Deathmoths Search

Directly in front of the now open door is a glass door that has an Elevator, but it has three locks on it, and the locks require Keys. There are hallways to the left and right in this area as well. Down the left hallway is a Dumbwaiter with a Note that says "iF YOU FEED THEM THEY WiLL REWARD YOU" and the option to put an Item inside to send down. Using the Bug Spray, you need to search the whole floor for three clusters of Male Deathmoths to get Moth Jelly. Use the Bug Spray on a cluster of Male Deathmoths and they will die and turn into Moth Jelly. Putting the Moth Jelly in the Dumbwaiter and sending it down will reward a Key when it comes back up, so you will need three things of the Moth Jelly. When you kill the last remaining Male Deathmoth, one more will spawn somewhere else, so don't worry if you accidentally eat a Moth Jelly. Using all three Keys on the locks on the glass door will open a hallway and at the end is an Elevator. Enter the Elevator and it will take you to the next floor: The Hotel.

Part 2: The Hotel

Find the Letters

This is where the terror begins. The Elevator will lead you to the Hotel, and once you make it to the floor, you will notice a Mailbox that has compartments for each main room. There is also a room labelled as Room 235, but there's a Lock on it with a 5-digit code. This room leads to the Boiler Room. The Mailbox rooms are all listed in order, and 7 of the boxes will be open (the open boxes are different every time) and you will need to travel to those rooms and collect papers that are on a drawer in the room. Once you collect a paper, you can place it in the mailbox, and it will automatically be placed in its position. Be careful, though, because the Hotel has a Skin-Stealer and a Hound roaming around. There's also more Male Deathmoths around the place, so if you still have some Bug Spray, you can get some Moth Jelly if you find any. Once you put all of the papers into the open Mailboxes, a code will appear on the ceiling. The papers spell out "LOOK UP", hinting at the ceiling code. Put the code into the Number Lock on the Room 235 door and it will open, allowing you to proceed onto The Boiler Room.

There are only 3 possible codes for the Number Lock, and these codes are:

  • 17564
  • 05938
  • 89472

Part 3: The Boiler Room

After entering the door to Room 235 and going up the stairs, you will end up in The Boiler Room. The room at the start is a small area with a table at the front that has 4 Thermometers and a Note. In order to leave The Boiler Room, you must navigate through the place and find the exit to Level 2. The Boiler Room is far from being safe, because the entire place is infested with Female Deathmoths and their nests. Using the Thermometer, you can find where the warm areas are. The warm areas are where the Female Deathmoths spawn the most. Moth Jelly puddles also appear on the ground nearby Female Deathmoths. If the Thermometer says anything other than 72 degrees, that means there's danger nearby. The Boiler Room also has 3 checkpoints around the Level in case you die. There are 4 total doors in The Boiler Room, and only one is the exit, so don't fall for the fake doors. Purple doors have Deathmoth Hives behind them. The real exit has an exit sign over it. Ignore any door without it.

extraSections.html: This level was one that was added on the game's Release. The level layout is the same as it is now except there wasn't the room that teaches you to sneak by the Partygoers. The Partygoers also had their original model. The other difference was the party room was an arcade room and the Level 37 entrance was completely different. The arcade room had the wall texture of the room that contains the blue key in Level Fun+. The arcade room had some arcade machines that Partygoers were playing, 2 long Tables, and Partygoers that were watching the Table on the left. You would have to quickly run into the Table before the Partygoers reached you and then go to the other side. Once on the other side, you would have to get out of the Table and quickly run over to the one on the right and then get to the middle of that Table. Then, you would have to make a run for the open hallway that has the normal Level Fun orange walls and get chased by the Partygoers. After running down the hallway for a bit, you would arrive at the exit room and the Partygoers wouldn't be able to enter. There was an LED Light, and all you had to do was touch it to be instantly sent to Level 37.

extraSections.html:
• The photo for the level is slightly outdated because the balloons still have older textures.

Behind it is a very dark room which contains two rows of Tables and Glowsticks sitting under them. This room was the old Level Fun arcade room that the level ended with in the game's Release. Part 2 changed this arcade room and the arcade room was stored behind the blue door. Currently, this room has no use, however this may change in the future.
  • The party room is reused from a found footage on YouTube.
  • The Release level selector description mentions a host, which may seem like a mention of Party Hosts, however this is unlikely considering the time between when Level Fun and Party Hosts were added to the game, and how Party Hosts are found in a way further level than Level Fun.
  • There is a Note that was found in the game files when Part 2 released that seemed like it would have been used in Level Fun. This Note was originally unused but returned in Level Fun+.
  • In Fancy's 1st Devlog, he said that he modeled this level after a place he used to go to. It is unknown what this place is for certain.
  • Fancy's goal with the Release version of Level Fun was basically to make it extremely hard, however this caused a lot of negative reception towards the level, so it was changed to how it is in Part 2.

items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: The First Half

Once you first spawn in, you're going to want to travel until you find a Table blocking the path. Crouch underneath this Table to get through it, and on the other side there is another Table. Pop the Balloons on this Table to attract the nearby Partygoer's attention and then hide underneath it. This should turn the Partygoer around that was previously looking straight at the pathway. Crouch your way past these nearby Partygoers until arriving at the hallway at the end. In the next room, you're going to want to head down the straightforward hallway and crouch behind a turned around Partygoer and stay next to the wall of the open area. Just don't go right, because that area is a trap, and don't go left before heading down the straightforward hallway.

Part 2: The Second Half

Upon getting to the next hallway, you'll come across a larger area with a lot of Partygoers around. Once you get into the room, head straight up from the hallway and at the end of the hallway, go right. You'll need to alert the Partygoers and go down the nearby hallway, which has a Table in it. Hide underneath the Table and head back out into the hallway. This time, run down the hallway where the Partygoers see you and go through the left hallway once reaching the end. Keep running down the hallway until you see a room on the right with 2 Tables. Hide underneath one. In the next area, you're going to want to crouch next to the right wall to avoid a Partygoer and get to the next hallway.

Part 3: The Party

This hallway connects to a room that looks completely different from the rest of the level. It appears much more colorful and has some Tables with items. There are no Partygoers here either. To escape the level, find the hallway with white tiles that's located in this room, and it will lead to a poolroom. The poolroom has 4 tube-shaped Slides that lead to the next level. Go down one (doesn't matter which one) and you'll arrive at Level 37.

There are two ways to die in this level. You would think you can't because of how safe this level is, but it is actually possible.

Dark-Tiled Rooms

Dark-Tiled Rooms are rooms that are very small, and have dark blue tiles, so you will need a Flashlight to see in one of these rooms. There are 3 of them in the level. The water in them is infected with the Hydrolitis Plague.

The Hot Water Zone

When going left in the intersection, you will find a spiral staircase that goes down to boiling hot water. If you enter this water, you will die.

Upon entering a Dark-Tiled Room, your screen would have static on it, the exact static that the Bacteria gives you when it is close. If you stayed in a Dark-Tiled Room for too long, you would die. In Part 4, the Hydrolitis Plague was added into the waters, but it was still possible to die to the rooms by standing on the edge of the Dark-Tiled Room outside of the water. extraSections.html:
There are two Tables in this level and two Fire Alarms you can use to stay alive in this level.

Tables

If you get caught by the Scratcher, you can make a run for a Table and hide underneath it. After waiting for a few seconds, the Scratcher will give up and leave you alone.

Fire Alarms

If you want to distract the Scratcher so you can collect some Tapes, you can use one of the two Fire Alarms in the level. The Fire Alarms are located on the complete opposite side of each other on the left and right side of the room. Use a Fire Alarm, and the Scratcher will go towards it, giving you time to collect the Tapes. Keep in mind that you can only use them so often and after using one, it takes 3 minutes to use them again. If you got caught by the Scratcher and want to use a Fire Alarm, the Fire Alarms won't do you any good.

The most notable difference was that once you turned on the Computer, a video would play that lasts about 2 minutes. It talks about visual form agnosia and how The World Insecure Unconscious Neuroscience was looking for participants to help them understand more about visual form agnosia. The Orb also spawned back then instead of the second room which took you to Level 9223372036854775807 after the video was played, or after it was finished.

extraSections.html:
• According to Fancy's Devlogs, this level is based off of the Fandom wiki Version and not the Wikidot Version.
  • This level in the game's Release was actually extremely hard due to the lack of Fire Alarms and being unable to hide under Tables meaning that if the Scratcher caught you, it was game over. The lights also didn't go out either.
  • Originally, Fancy thought this was the last level of the Backrooms, but then everyone told him about Level 9223372036854775807. After making that level, everybody then said that the actual final level is Level 3999.

items.summary: The End creates the illusion of escaping the Backrooms. items.walkthroughHtml: Once you first spawn in, you will find yourself in a box-like library room, with the only possible exit being a locked door. To unlock it, go around the level and collect Tapes you will find on bookshelves, avoiding the Scratcher that aimlessly roams the room. The Computer turns on once you spawn into the level and it has a number on it of how many Tapes need to be collected. The amount of Tapes needed depends on how many people you're playing with. If it's one person, there is 24 Tapes to be collected, if it's 2 people, there is 32 Tapes to be collected, and if it's 3 or 4 people, there is 48 Tapes to be collected. Once all of the Tapes are collected, go up to the Computer on the center desk and turn it back on. This will unlock the door, which leads to a much more damaged room with a Ladder. Enter the room and climb up the Ladder to reach a Vent that leads to Level 94.

items.appearanceHtml: Level 94 is a vast expanse of dream-like green hills filled with different sorts of objects that a young kid would draw, including a water tower, houses as well as fences around them. The level also has a dreamcore vibe. The sky is mainly bright with the sun shining down on the level until nighttime. Trucks can be found on some of the streets that go throughout Level 94, and there are massive Sirens that play music that sounds as if it would come from an old 1900's cartoon.

extraSections.html: This level possesses a day/night cycle. Day lasts for 3 minutes and night lasts for 1 minute. But after the first night, day will last for 2 minutes. The day is perfectly safe while at night, hordes of Animations will come to kill anyone unfortunate enough to not find a house to hide inside of in time. The Animations will also break down doors of houses if they figure out you're in there, so hiding in one of the 2 Closets or under the Bed is the safest thing to do. When it becomes nighttime, the music from the Sirens will stop and Trucks will close their doors regardless if you are in them or not.

extraSections.title: History

extraSections.html:
• This was the last level in the game in the game's Release.
    • After completing Level 9223372036854775807, a cutscene would play of a man waking up at a house and then going over to the window and see that outside is Level 94 (the early level 94). Then, the credits would roll. This Ending was brought back in a similar way with The Suburbs Ending.
  • This is currently the largest level in the game.
  • Level 94 has a total of 126 houses you can find.
  • There are locked doors in the second floors of every house, however only the one in the first house works and has a Hub Door behind it. All of the doors actually have a room behind them, but there's nothing in them.
  • There are two houses close to the Animated Kingdom in this level that are inside of each other.
  • You can beat this level right as it turns night for the first time if you get to the Animated Kingdom fast enough with really good movement.
  • Prior to Version 1.0, Level 9223372036854775807 was the entry point to this level.
  • In Fancy's 2nd Devlog, it was revealed that this level was going to be a secret level. This didn't end up happening, and it ended up becoming a level that requires you to complete to progress. Fancy could have also just called it a secret level because he was working on it secretly.
  • In Fancy's 3rd Devlog, he mentioned how he used a height map to create the landscape.
  • Before this level released, it looked very different and had different house designs. There were also no Roads, Trucks, Sirens, Water Tower, and Animated Kingdom.
  • In the pre-release version of Level 94, instead of Animations, countless amounts of Smilers would spawn, trapping you inside a house until the Sun came back up. This was changed due to people wanting Animations instead of Smilers.
  • The sound of the gate closing after entering the miniature amusement park is the same sound used for the gate opening sound in Level 3, but both levels have 2 separate audio files for both sounds.
  • Just like Level 6 and Level 0.11, pictures of this level were used on Level 55's page on the Backrooms Fandom wiki, although the pictures originate from an unofficial Escape the Backrooms Wiki and as a result are credited to it.
    • Something interesting about pictures of Level 94 being used for a page of Level 55 is that a level called Level 55.1 exists in the game but bears zero resemblance to Level 94.
extraSections.title: Trivia

items.name: Level 94
items.summary: Level 94 appears to be a large town, a floating castle and grass hills, but everything has a grainy effect, as if the place was crafted. The main town is safe, with no Entities during the day. Many survivors have described it as looking like a suburban neighborhood in Ohio.
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Get to the Castle

Once you first spawn in, there will be a sign pointing right. On this sign, there is a Note warning you about Animations. Traverse through Level 94 and hide in a house at night so you don't die to Animations and use Trucks to gain distance. Get in a Truck and it will start up, taking you for a little ride to get you closer and closer to the castle that you have to get to. The Trucks won't work forever though. In order to escape the neighborhood, you must reach the Animated Kingdom and climb the rope coming down from it to enter.

Part 2: The Animated King's Trial

Once you're in the castle, you will find yourself in a funhouse place with a locked door at the front of the room. In this funhouse, there are 4 Cubes that you can push around with shapes on them. One with a star, one with a circle, one with a square, and one with all of them. Push these Cubes into their place on the floor that has the same shape. If you get it correctly, the door will unlock. The door will close after entering the area. You will then enter a miniature amusement park with stuff like tables, and lampshades. In the center, there is a podium with the Animated King on it. Once looking away from it for a few seconds, he will disappear. Then the boss battle will begin. The lights will turn off and the Animated King will attempt to attack you when you're not looking at him, and when you do see him, he will disappear with some time until his next attack. There are also Liquid Pain dispensers, which every now and then will spawn Liquid Pain. Once another one has more Liquid Pain, the previous one's Liquid Pain will disappear. Survive for 100 seconds (1 minute and 40 seconds) and get on the Roller Coaster in order to escape and get to Level 6.

  • In Fancy's 1st Devlog, he said that making the Backrooms levels in order would get repetitive and a lot of the levels are pretty boring, mentioning Level 6, Level 7, and Level 10. Ironically though, all three of these levels have been added to the game.
  • In Fancy's 3rd Devlog, it was mentioned that this level was originally going to have a mechanic where you would have to listen to sounds to escape, but this for some reason got replaced by the LiDAR Scanner.
  • It was hard for Fancy to find references on what the level looks like due to nearly every image of the level just being a black screen. When he did find a reference image, it was designed into the room at the start of the level.
  • The LiDAR Scanner was added because roaming around the level with no idea where you are and where to go would make a pretty annoying level.

items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: The Flashlight Area

When you first spawn in, you will find yourself in a dark room with the only light coming from 2 rooms on the side and an entrance to the rest of the level. The left room has a ton of Almond Water, Juice, and Energy Bars, while the right room has 4 Flashlights and some items on the ground. Both rooms have windows and are 2 of the only four rooms to have some sort of light. When you enter the rest of the level, you will need to use a Flashlight to navigate the flashlight area. There are also some rooms here that contain items you can find. Look for a room that has a flickering light to move on.

Part 2: The Maze

Once you find the room, you will also find a hallway that continues on to a pitch-black labyrinth where Flashlights don't work. You need to use a LiDAR Scanner from the flickering light room on the left to shoot dots onto the walls and navigate the maze. LiDAR Scanners also have trackers so you can see where the Wretch roaming the level is. Find a short hallway with a light coming through a door to get to Level 7.

items.appearanceHtml: Level 7 is an ocean that seems to stretch on for miles. There are houses all around the ocean and one that has a lighthouse on top of it and a Shark Tank connected to it. The ocean has sand across the floor, and rocks all over the place.

items.subtitle: Thalassophobia
items.summary: Level 7 is an impossibly large ocean that seems to stretch infinitely in all directions. Despite the absence of fixed light sources within the level, a dim natural light is present across the level. Detailed exploration of this ocean is sparse due to the extreme danger and amount of preparation it requires to navigate.
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Follow the Light

When you first spawn in, you'll be in a regular house with some items everywhere, and the downstairs of the house is flooded with dirty water. A window downstairs is your only way out of this house, which leads to the rest of the ocean. There are 4 Boats around the house. Get in a Boat and start traveling. Go from house to house and get some Items the houses have to offer. One of the houses at the beginning has some Flare Guns inside and a Note next to them. Use them to your advantage! At a certain point in the ocean, fog will appear and the lighthouse at the final house will activate after a few minutes. Follow the light in order to get to the final house. This house has a Shark Tank connected to it and four Diving Helmets next to it. Put on a Diving Helmet and enter the Tank.

Part 2: Find the Cave

Once in the Tank, you will go down into the ocean. When going down, The Thing on Level 7 will try to get you, and it ends up breaking the Tank forcing you to swim around the bottom of the ocean. When swimming around, look for an entrance to a Cave inside of a rock structure. There are lights inside, so you know that it's where you need to go. Enter the Cave and follow the path to arrive at Level 8.

items.subtitle: Cave System
items.summary: Level 8 is made of huge caverns and small cave systems that twist and turn like normal underground systems.
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Sneak past the Deathmoths

Once you first spawn in, you will find yourself at the entrance. There is a table with some Items as well as a new Item: Walkie Talkies. Make sure to get a Flashlight to navigate the place better. Enter the transporter and turn the valve to turn it on. Bug Spray will come out, and then the door will open, sending you into the cave. In the first two areas, you must sneak past Female Deathmoths that are around the place. Look around until you find the next transporter. All the transporters look the same, so make sure you're in the right one.

Part 2: The Bridge

The second area looks the same, but you must find a bridge. The bridge is not in good condition due to its believed age, so running on it will result in it to break and kill you. Either walk or crouch on it so it doesn't break. Jumping on it can also result in it to break. On the other side, there is a table with some Items on it as well as the next transporter. Enter the transporter to get sent to the third area. If you do not want to take the bridge to get across, there is another path you can take. To the right of the bridge start and up the rocks is a tunnel which will take you to the other side and past the transporter. However, it is not a good idea to take this path if as it will prevent you from getting the Items on the table on the other side of the bridge and you won't be able to hide from Skin-Stealers in the transporter.

Part 3: Survive the Skin-Stealers

In the third area, you have to survive two Skin-Stealers that have Flashlights. This area is the same as before, just find the transporter to arrive at the final area.

Part 4: Get to the Other Side

In the final area, you'll find a ditch that will stop your journey. You must climb a ladder that is in this final area to get back to Level 0. In Level 0, you will be in the smaller area of the Level, and you must complete Level 0.11 and take the Elevator in the Level 188 Courtyard. Once the Elevator ride is done you will be on the other side of the ditch (the ditch is blocked off by invisible walls). Take the available path and go up the pathway that it leads to, and you'll arrive at Level 9.

extraSections.html:
• This level has gone through many changes. Originally, this level was actually the rest of the Level 188 courtyard, and Fancy thought it looked too generic. Then, he added Level 🗿 but just ended up making Level 0.11 instead because of how similar they look. However, the Moai Statues were still included throughout the Level in open areas. After a while of the Statues being in the level, he thought they looked absolutely ridiculous, and then he just deleted them all. The statues still somehow made a small cameo in the Level 0.11 layer in Level 55.1, and they are one of the things you must take a picture of in the Sub-Level.
  • In Multiplayer, the host is always the one that will spawn at the player spawn in Singleplayer.
  • The reason why you don't get an Achievement for completing this Sub-Level is because of the original idea that this level was originally going to be the rest of the Level 188 courtyard.
  • When Part 3 came out, the Level 188 courtyard was originally Level 188, but in Part 4, a completed Level 188 was added, making the Level 188 courtyard a part of Level 0.11.
  • The fireplace in the hotel area in the Level 188 courtyard doesn't have sound, but fans on Level 1 do have sound when getting close to them.
  • The ambience for this level is the same as Level 0's Ambience, but with more unnerving sounds included.
  • The Level 188 courtyard is connected to the level because it does not have a loading screen.
  • Just like Level 94 and Level 6, the main render of this level and a picture from this wiki page were used on a rewrite of Level 0.11's page on the Backrooms Fandom wiki.

items.name: Level 0.11
items.summary: Level 0.11 looks just like Level 0 but it's extremely rundown and abandoned. The level also has rooms that contain furniture such as chairs and tables. There's also a smaller version of this level in the final layer in Level 55.1 .
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Escape the Maze

Once you first spawn in, you will find yourself in a maze with no way to go back to Level 0. Look around for Spray-Painted arrows on the walls and follow them to escape the maze.

Part 2: Unlock the Gate

After escaping the maze, you will find yourself in a much smaller area that resembles a bunch of hallways with pillars and doors. These doors will appear to have boards on them, so use a Chainsaw to destroy the boards and enter the room. The objective at this point is to find 4 Levers in these rooms and flip them all, but they will slowly unwind which means if you take too long, you will have to flip a Lever again. Keep an eye out for the Bacteria as well, because it is roaming around this level. If it spots you, you have to find a Vent and hide in it so it leaves you alone. Flip all 4 Levers and go to the gate beyond the main area. If all of the Levers are flipped, the gate should be fully open. On the other side, there is a door that leads to the Level 188 Courtyard, and a Lever that is used to open the gate again which is useless in singleplayer but can come in handy when playing with multiple players multiplayer.

Part 3: The Courtyard

Once you enter the Level 188 courtyard, proceed forward and enter the hotel area. This small hotel area has an Elevator that will let you leave the level, but it has a Color-Coded Lock. Look at the windows and the layout of the open windows is the code. The code is always the same and it is 415314. Type this into the keypad, and the Elevator should open. Enter the Elevator and press the buttons in it, and you will be back at Level 8.

extraSections.html:
• This level took Fancy multiple months to make, which explains why it's so long.
  • People really seemed to like this level when it came out due to its length and quality.
  • Even though this level is pretty small in size, it still is one of the longest to complete.
  • This level was one of the most teased by Fancy.
    • There was a teaser of the level, but it was blurred. The location of this teaser is unknown.
    • There was a video showing The Neighborhood Watch.
    • And there was a teaser of one of the 20+ houses you can explore in the level.
  • The Note you can find in this level can be found twice.
  • Level 9 has the highest Sanity risk in the entire game.
  • This level has the most Items that can be found in the entire game, beating Level 5 by one Item (unless you count each color of Glowstick in Level Fun+ as a different Item).
  • Upstairs, in the center house, you can find a bedroom with a red light. This might be a reference to Level 974, since it's a pink house and has you start in a bedroom, but it could also possibly be a reference to Jerry, an entity that was going to be in the level but got removed.
  • The Moon in this level is actually fully 3D.
  • There are 25 houses in this level (including the big house in the center).
  • There are three explorable houses that cannot have a Computer. Two are safe, and the third has a Wretch. (One of the safe ones is the big house)
  • There is a strategy where you lead a Wretch to the door and close it. This will push it outside their barrier. It can't go back in.
extraSections.title: Trivia

items.summary: Level 9 is a suburban area at night with many houses that vary in design and size. There have been reports of an outbreak in the M.E.G. base located in the level but it cannot be confirmed.
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Turn on the Terminals

Once you first spawn in, explore the neighborhood until you find the big house at the top of the level. This house will be guarded off with a fence, as it stores The Abandoned Outpost inside. There will be a Terminal including a mini map of the level, with 3 different houses highlighted red (the houses will be different every time). Go to these houses and find the Computers inside of them. Turn them on, and make sure to be careful, because there might be a Wretch in a house. Once they're all on, go back to the big house and the gate will be unlocked. In the house, find the Spray-Painted arrow pointing at a door. Enter this door, and you will arrive at The Abandoned Outpost.

Part 2: Get the Administrator Card

In The Abandoned Outpost, you must find 4 bottles of Almond Concentrate and use them to return the Wretch who is roaming around the place back to normal. With this, you can get the administrator card it has and leave the Outpost. With the card, go to the back of the level. You should find a gate with a Administrator Card Terminal next to it. Use the Administrator Card on the Terminal to unlock the gate and get to Level 10.

items.appearanceHtml: Level 10 is a very large and extensive wheat field. It has windmills scattered around the level and a barn. There are lakes in the level which act as landmarks. The majority of the level is wheat and fences, with a patch of corn next to the barn.

extraSections.html:
• This level is very disliked due its tedious nature.
  • You can skip to the end of this level by crouch jumping from a haystack onto the fence, which will allow you to walk on top of the fence all the way to the Arcade.
  • This level in the first teaser was shown to have seemingly a blue sky, making it feel safe. But in the final version, the level has dark, grey fog that completely hides the original nice blue sky, making it feel not so safe anymore.
  • Level 10 was first revealed in August of 2022, and released on June 15th, 2023 in Part 3 almost a year after its existence in the game was first revealed, making it the Level that has taken the second most time to make. The level that took the longest time to make is Level 974.
  • In Hotfix #14, an extra Windmill was added to the level.
  • The layout of this level was originally different, but it was slightly changed to how it is now in Hotfix #15.
  • Originally, this level was apparently just supposed to be the road at the start of the level, but it became much more after Fancy read further into the Backrooms Fandom Wiki page for this level.
  • In Fancy's 1st Devlog, he said that making the Backrooms levels in order would get repetitive and a lot of the levels are pretty boring, mentioning Level 6, Level 7, and Level 10. Ironically though all three of these levels have still ended up in the game.
  • Fancy considered this level as a bad level because of the level being really boring, but important to lore. He also said in his 8th Devlog that he'll never add a bad level again after hearing the response to Level 10.

items.summary: Level 10 is an expansive field of wheat that stretches endlessly in all directions. The climate of the level appears to never change from a foggy, dreary sky, with only the occasional gust of wind among the crops to break up the uniformity.
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Stalker in the Road

Upon spawning in, you'll be on a road. Simply walk down the road until you arrive at a barn, but the walk won't be easy. Every now and then, be sure to look back and make sure the Stalker Faceling isn't sneaking up on your team. Upon spotting it, it will return into the fields. If it gets to whoever it targets, it will take them into the wheat fields never to be seen again.

Part 2: Lighting the way out

Upon arrival to the barn, go through it, and you'll arrive at what seems to be the end. An opening that leads to the wheat, but you can actually walk through the wheat. Scattered throughout the tall wheat field is 5 Windmills the next one to enable having a red light shining through the fog to guide wanderers to it. These red lights will become green upon finding the Windmill and interacting with the generator inside of it, which will enable the red light of the next Windmill. After activating the first Windmill and heading to the next wheat opening, a Faceling with a Chainsaw will come out of it and start the Chainsaw. After this, the Farmer Faceling will always be hunting you down but will spawn at a different place closer to you or your team depending on what checkpoint the level is loaded from, and the only way to avoid it is by running away. To easily survive traversing the tall wheat, it's best to stick to fences so you can easily get to openings for your first few playthroughs. Enable all the Windmills until arriving at the fifth Windmill, which is beside the parking lot of Level 3999 and is not required to enable since you've already arrived at the Arcade. Enter the Arcade to get to Level 3999.

items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Get to the Mini Arcade

Once you first spawn in, you will spawn in the entrance. Go through the doors at the start of the Level, and on the other side will be a long, damaged, abandoned and rundown arcade hall.

Part 2: The Mini Arcade

At the end of it is a small room with a bunch of arcade games. There's an Elevator with a code, and the code is somewhere within the room. At the top right corner of the room, there is a table with several Tickets. The Tickets display the number 931. Type in this code on the Elevator and it will activate. On the trip a video from The M.E.G. Civilization will play on a TV suspended in a ceiling corner. It talks about being aware of Noclipping and how the Traffic Lights in front of the Noclipping floors work in Level 0.2. If you don't want to watch it you can press any of the elevator buttons to skip it, but the game doesn't recommend it. The video will not replay in the same save file, so it is not a good idea to skip it. Once the video is done (or you skipped it), the elevator will arrive at Level 0.2.

items.walkthroughHtml:
The M.E.G. Civilization

This is what you have to do in The M.E.G. Civilization.

Part 1: Turn on the Electricity Boxes

Once you first spawn in, you will be in a room with walls and carpet that resembles Level 0.2. Next to this room is a large area with a hole in the wall. This hole is where the Bone Thief lives. The exit to the level is on the other side of the Gate, however the Bone Thief will stop you from getting to it. To get to the Elevator, You must enter the rest of the level and look for Noclipping floors which are glitching areas in the ground. They are all marked with yellow tape and have Traffic Lights next to them. If the Traffic Light is green, that means there's no danger nearby the Noclipping floor, but if it's red, that means there's danger nearby and you should wait for it to turn green. Once you walk through a Noclipping floor, you will fall through and enter Level 0.1. Within the rest of the level, there are 5 doors, and they are all locked. In order to unlock them, you must go into Level 0.1 and open up the doors. If you've opened up a door, return to The M.E.G. Civilization and open whichever door is unlocked. On the other side is a small room, and each room looks different. They all contain Electricity Boxes which must be enabled to escape. On the Electricity Box is a moving arrow and a yellow area. You must stop the arrow in the yellow area to complete the puzzle. Turn on all 5 of the Electricity Boxes and return to the Bone Thief Area.

Part 2: Get to the Elevator

After turning on all 5 of the Electricity Boxes, the lever next to the Bone Thief area will be usable. Once pulled, a Gate will come down and it will keep the Bone Thief from getting out, however the gate will go back up once it reaches the bottom meaning you'll have to be quick to get to the Elevator. Once in the Elevator, it will close and go down. The Elevator leads to The Snackrooms.

Level 55.1

This is what you have to do in the Level 0.2 layer in Level 55.1.

Part 1: Who is Here?

Once you get to the layer from Level 55.1, you must have a Camera with you, and you must find a wall that says, "Who is Here?" in the level. Once you find it, take a picture of it and the photo on the Camera will change to a room that has a view of Level 0. If you are playing in Multiplayer, the "Who is Here?" photo will be a photo of another player.

Part 2: The Level 0 Entrance

After taking the first photo, you must take a photo of an area that has a view of Level 0. Find the area and take the photo. The photo will then change to a damaged van in Level 0.3. You can then go to the Level 0.3 Layer to continue Level 55.1.

items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Finding the Clump

Once you first spawn in, you will notice a gate blocking off the rest of the Level. In the left hallway lies a Pressure Plate, and in the right hallway lies another Gate. Stand on the Pressure Plate, and the gate in the right hallway will open. Proceed down the hallway and after a right turn will be a Lever on the left hall and forward is a dark hallway. Pull the lever, and a Clump will come out of the darkness.

Part 2: RUN

Turn around and the gate that blocked off the rest of the Level before will be open. Enter the hallway and keep running. Doors on the left and right will start opening, so make sure to avoid those. As you're running, you'll eventually arrive at a big room with 2 hallways and a table in the center. The left hallway is a blue hallway, and the right one is a red one. If you enter the red hallway, a Smiler will come after you if you go too far down. Enter the blue hallway, and the Clump will enter the red hallway. In the blue hallway, there is a Chainsaw on a table, so make sure to pick it up. Go through the door in the blue hallway, and on the other side of it is a hole. Jump down, and you'll fall down the hole. Once you fall down, immediately turn around because you'll be facing an open hallway that the Clump will be in. On the other side is a boarded-up door. Use the Chainsaw on the door and make sure to be quick because the Clump will be right behind you. After 2 uses of the Chainsaw, the door will be openable. Go through the door and you'll arrive at a big room with balconies. Go through the left or right side and get to the door on the other side, however you could also jump across the table in the middle. Keep running until you arrive at a small room with tables with chairs on them.

Go around the tables and enter the next hallway. The next room you'll arrive at is a kitchen with a hole in the ground. A counter is in the middle of the room, so make sure to go around it on the right. Go through the hole the arrow points at. You will then fall through a blue hallway and instantly be back at the red ones. On the right hallway is the Clump, so you will have to go forward. Keep going until you arrive at a hallway that splits off into 2. The left direction is the way to go. You will then arrive at a room with an object blocking the way. Go around it and go left. The next room is a staircase that goes down for a while. Go down the stairs until you spot an open door. You can keep going down the stairs after that, but you will go the wrong way. Enter the open door and go right in it. You will then spot a hallway that goes up and goes left, but there's also a hallway that is lower and goes right. If you take the right hallway, another Smiler will come after you if you go too far down, and a fake blue hallway can be found. The door to it will close if you try to go near it. The right hallway is the right way to go. Keep going through the right hallway and eventually, you'll find another area that cuts off into 2 hallways. This time, you must go right. Keep going forward in this hallway, and you will find a staircase that goes up to a blue hallway. This is the final blue hallway in the Level, and once you enter it the Clump will leave you alone. Enter the door on the left hallway the blue hallway leads to, and on the other side is Level 188.

extraSections.html:
• The Courtyard in this Level is designed after a real place, the Holiday Inn at Terminal 4 of Heathrow Airport in London England.
  • A smaller version of this Level can be found in Level 0.11 at the end of the Level.
  • The Courtyard has 114 windows, which is not enough to fill out all the Levels in the game with them since Fancy mentioned in his 7th Devlog that every window would eventually be another Level for him to add.
    • The game currently only has 33 major Levels, which means if you started filling out the windows with the Levels on the left or right side, you wouldn't even be able to complete either of the sides since both of them have 42 windows each. The front side only has 30 windows however, which is more than how many Levels there are in the game.
  • You won't get the Escape Level 188 Achievement for completing Level 188 because it is given for escaping Level 0.11 since Level 188 was once the Level 188 Courtyard in Level 0.11.
  • Originally, Level 0.11 was entirely going to look like Level 188 instead of just having the Courtyard at the end, but later on Fancy thought it looked way too boring and changed it to look like Level 0.11 but never changed the last area featuring the Courtyard likely to keep them as 2 separate Levels. In Part 4, he added what is now entirely Level 188.
  • Sometimes the Elevator will take you to floor 3 of The Hotel. This can happen randomly. floor 3 is identical to floor 2, and you will be brought up to the higher balcony when you get there.
  • If you are holding an Item in front of a bathroom mirror, you will only see the player's hands.

items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Collect the TV's

Once you first spawn in, you will be in the lobby. Go forward until you find an open Elevator. Get in the Elevator and use the buttons to go up and down a floor. The first floor is The Lobby, and the second is The Hotel. Go to The Hotel and look through the hotel rooms to find TV's. Each TV is labelled with 3 different letters: A, B, and C. The TV's can be found in bathrooms, in the open areas of The Hotel, and in hotel rooms. The Aranea Membri is roaming around the halls though, so if it spots you, make sure to hide in a hotel room. The Window Entity can also be seen in the hotel rooms. The TV's can be moved around very slowly, and once one is obtained, you must move it to the start and into an elevator next to the entry one with Electric Puddles in them. The TV will enter the Elevator and the Elevator will go down to The Lobby. Once going down to The Lobby, the TV will be out of the Elevator. You must then move it over to where the desk is, and below the 3 windows that show the very small courtyard are indicators for where to put each TV. Put all 3 down and the metal door that leads to the very small courtyard will open.

Part 2: The Very Small Courtyard

The very small courtyard features a hole and if you decide to go forward beyond the hole, a small area can be found with a door, which leads to Level You Cheated. If you try to go here, the Aranea Membri will come down from a Vent and start chasing you, so you will have to jump down the hole which leads to Level 37.2. In order to access the door, you can hide behind railings after activating the Aranea Membri and enter the level.

items.summary: Level 37.2 looks mostly like Level 37 but with new locations, darker areas, and much more danger.
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Entering the Expanded Poolrooms

Once you first spawn in, you will find yourself in a big room with windows, and a hole that you must enter to proceed onto the rest of the Level. When you enter the next area, there will be a pool that has the Hydrolitis Plague. Then you will find a Floatie Boat. Get in the Boat and start travelling. Shortly in, the path will have no more water, which means you will have to take a ladder to go up to the Valves. There are 4 Valves, and all you need to do is turn them all on. They are on the right side as well. Once you turn on all the Valves, the path will fill with water, and you will be able to continue on. You will then go down a very fast water path. In the next area, the water will be too low to continue. There will be a nearby ladder, and when you get up, the entrance to the first Dark Poolrooms area will be accessible. There will be 3 ladders, and you will arrive in the first Dark Poolrooms area after going down any one of them.

Part 2: Visiting the Dark Poolrooms

In the Dark Poolrooms, you must go around and turn 4 Valves in order to fill up a tank with water in it. There are also rooms that contain grass, fake outside walls, and giant mushrooms. These rooms resemble Level -33.5 from the Spanish Backrooms Wiki. Each area has one of these rooms. Just make sure you keep an eye out for the Clumps in the Level. If it spots you, just hide in the water and he will leave you alone. Once all of the Valves are turned, you can go back to the Expanded Poolrooms.

Part 3: Back on the Surface

When you get back to the Expanded Poolrooms, the water will be filled, and you will be able to get back in the Floatie Boat and continue on. However, shortly into travelling, you run into another obstacle, which is a giant pit. You must then once again go up the ladder on the left or right and turn all the valves (this time there's 4 on the left and the right) there's also a bridge that will let you easily get to the other side. However, if you run on it, it will break so be careful. Once all the Valves are turned, the pit will be filled, and you can get back in the Floatie Boat and continue travelling. However, soon enough, there will be another obstacle. The path cuts off, so you must get the boat to the other side by lowering the water level. There will be another ladder that you must go up and the entrance to the second Dark Poolrooms area will be there.

Part 4: Back to the Dark Poolrooms

The Second Dark Poolrooms area is similar to the first one, but with a different map layout and with 3 Valves instead of 4. It's the easiest area. However, there is Hydrolitis Plague in some parts of the Level. Turn all 3 valves and leave.

Part 5: Back to the Expanded Poolrooms

After leaving the second Dark Poolrooms area, go through the triangular entrance to get to the next part of The Expanded Poolrooms. There will be a hole, and inside is the Floatie Boat. After travelling for a bit, you will run into yet again another obstacle. The water won't be high enough to proceed. So, you will have to take the nearby ladder, and then you will have to take the stairs and then you will arrive at the above part of this area. In this above part, the entrance to the third Dark Poolrooms area will be there.

Part 6: Down to the Dark Poolrooms Again

You know how it works. Same as last time, but just a different map layout. Except this time there are 7 valves instead of 4. Turn all 7 and leave.

Part 7: Escape!

Once you return to the Expanded Poolrooms, the water will be high enough to continue. At this point, you will no longer need the boat, so just get on the stairs and on the other side is a pool with 3 entrances to a big room. This big room has a bunch of colored slides with a staircase leading up to them. It also has a beach ball, and life savers from the original Level 37. Go up the staircase and at the top is a room with all 4 of the slides. Enter one of the slides (doesn't matter which one) and you will find yourself in Level Fun+.

items.appearanceHtml: Level Fun+ is an open party area with many plastic Tables and chairs present throughout the level. Unlike Level Fun, this level has different colored walls, tons of areas, and more open ones. The floor is covered by a carpet made for little kids, and there are 4 sectors that you must go to in order to escape. The Level is also the home of the leader of the Partygoers: The Party Host.

extraSections.html:
The Sublevel

  • This level has the most Note variations in the entire game with a total of 5.
  • For some reason, this level does not have an Achievement for completing it or a Hub Entry.

In-Game Information

Details

  • The wall design of the room with the blue key is the same as the wall texture of the room behind the locked blue door/the old Arcade room at the end of Level Fun.

Community

  • This level won the Backrooms Bracket, an event hosted by the game's publisher Secret Mode on the Escape the Backrooms Discord server to determine which level is the best in the whole game.

Media & References

  • The billiard room in the Arcade Sector might be a reference to Buckshot Roulette, as they are both on the second floor of a party type setting and feature a billiard board in the room. Buckshot Roulette was also released before Part 4 was, which makes it more likely.

Game Files

  • The Note in the Party Sector was found in the game files of Part 2 and was most likely going to have been used for the locked door at the end of Level Fun, although in Part 4, it was moved to Level Fun+.

Production Facts

  • This level was added because of how small Level Fun is.
  • The Partypoopers were most likely originally going to appear in this level but were moved to Level 52.

Sublevel Inspiration

  • This is the only level in the game from the Backrooms Freewriting Wiki.
