items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: The Party Room

Once you first spawn in, you will be in a ball pit and connected to it will be the Party Room. The Party Room contains a bunch of Tables with chairs along with 4 different colored hallways. Each hallway leads to a different sector. In every sector, there will be a key that you must obtain to escape. The order of the sectors you go into doesn't matter as you just need all of the keys.

Part 2: Getting Used to the Sectors

The sectors you can find include:

  • The Party Sector (the bright blue hallway with clouds on the wall)
  • The Playhouse Sector (the pink hallway with clouds on the wall)
  • The Arcade Sector (the red hallway)
  • The Funhouse Sector (the dark blue hallway with plastic balls)
  • The Party Sector
  • The Playhouse Sector
  • The Arcade Sector
  • The Funhouse Sector
The Party Sector is a massive area with a bunch of walls, Tables, and a large room in the middle containing tables, chairs, and a different wall design. Your goal here is to go around and pop all of the Balloons scattered around because one of them will contain the yellow key. However, the Balloon the yellow key will be in a different room each time. The sector has 3 Partygoers roaming around, so keep an eye out for them. The Playhouse Sector starts off with rooms similar to the original Level Fun, just much smaller. In here you can find a big square room with Balloons everywhere, and a hallway that connects to 3 rooms. These rooms look just like nostalgic houses, and you must find a Tape in one of the 3 rooms. The Tapes only have 1 spawn for each room, so it shouldn't be too hard to find. Once you have the Tape, go back to the room with the Balloons and insert the Tape into the TV. The video shows a Balloon icon, and then the color green will flash on the screen. You must then simply pop a green Balloon. Then, it will do it again, but after green it will show yellow. Then, you have to pop a green and then yellow Balloon. The next color it will show is blue. And then it will show Red. And lastly, pink. If you get a color wrong, all of the Balloons will respawn and you will have to start over. Once all of the Balloons are popped, the secured vault door will open, and inside is a room very similar to the original photo of Level Fun. There is a wooden Table in this room as well as artwork of Partygoers and Partypoopers. On the wooden table is the green key. The Arcade Sector is a neon-type area with an area that features a bunch of golf courses, and also some tables that contain Glowsticks as well as a Bowling Alley. In the Golf Area, there are 4 Glowstick tables, and each one has a different color glowstick. The colors include green, blue, red, and yellow. You will need one of every color as well as an extra one. Once you've collected all of the Glowsticks you need, go up the stairs and enter the dark zone. Get out one of the Glowsticks to brighten up the place, as Flashlights do not work in the Zone, and then you must go around the darkness and find 4 different symbols. 3 of the symbols are on the walls in the halls, and one of them is in a closet in one of the rooms. The symbols include balloons, a present, an eye, and a rainbow. The rainbow is the one stored in the closet. Once you find a symbol, you must place down the correct Glowstick right below it. When you are holding the correct Glowstick, the Symbol will light up. The Balloons need a green Glowstick, the present will need a red one, the eye will need a yellow one, and the rainbow will need a blue one. There is a Partygoer roaming around the dark zone, so keep an eye out for it. Once all of the Glowsticks are placed down, you can leave the dark zone and go to the locked door, and it will be open. Inside will be the blue key.

The Funhouse Sector is a cluster of rooms with different fun children's activities in them like trampolines, a rock-climbing wall, and more. The room you start off in is fairly normal and appears to be a school classroom. There's a locked blue door and steel gray doors that have bars around them, and on the roof of this classroom lies a Party Host. In order to move on, you must kill it. In order to kill it, grab a Firework from a table and throw it at the Party Host. Then, the blue door will be unlocked. You will then find yourself in a much bigger room with a wooden pathway as well as other segments. There are more steel doors, but there are no longer bars around them. The next Party Host is also above the doors. Every now and then, Partygoers will come out of the doors, or if the Party Host spots you they'll come out so killing the Party Hosts now won't be that easy. Once you kill the Party Host, the next room has a rock-climbing wall in the middle, and the Party Host is on the top right of the room. Once again, beware of the Partygoers. Once you kill the Party Host, you must go into the room on the right side of the room first.

This room has the Party Host above a box with a bunch of presents, and letters reading "HAPPY BIRTHDAY". There are no steel doors in this room, however the Partygoers will still come from the previous room. After killing the Party Host, you can go into the room on the left side. This room is a ball pit with balloons on the wall. There are also pillars, and there actually is a steel door. The Party Host in this room is on the ceiling. Kill it, and then you can move onto the next room. The doors to proceed on will be at the top of the room, and this next area is the hardest in this sector. It features a pathway to walk on and a ball pit. The pathway will lead to the Party Host right above the steel door. After killing the Party Host, you can go up a slope that leads down into an area that doesn't have a ball pit, but does have a trampoline and structural support. The Party Host here is quite high, because it's on the ceiling. Make sure to keep an eye on the steel door as well. Kill the Party Host, and a room on the bottom right of this area will unlock that has a ball pit, a slide to go down into the ball pit, and the red key. Collecting the Red Key will make the Sector safe to traverse.

Part 3: Opening the Gate

Once you have all 4 of the Keys, you can go back to the main area and go up to a Key Machine. In the Key Machine, you must move around the thing in the Machine and go to the Keyhole that corresponds with the color of the Key it has out. Once that is done, insert the key into the hole, and another key will pop out. The previous one will fall down. Now, you must go to the keyhole that corresponds with that key. Then, the key will be swapped out again. And then again. However, after using the last key, the exit gate will open, and you can get to Level 52.

items.summary: Level 52 is a infinite school consisting of hallways, cafeterias, and classrooms similar to a school in the frontrooms.
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Find the Partypooper

Once you first spawn in, you will be in the starting class, which is a room with pillars and desks that contain items. There are also doors that lead to the rest of the level. Proceed through these doors and go through the hallway.

Part 2: Follow the Partypooper

Shortly into the Level, you will find a Partypooper. Once getting close to him, he will start moving. Follow the Partypooper, as he will guide you to the exit. However, if he crouches, you must crouch as well because it means that there is a Partygoer nearby. If he is standing up, that means it's safe. If you don't crouch when the Partypooper crouches, the Partygoers will come after you. After a short while of travelling through the school with the Partypooper, you will arrive at the exit. The Partypooper will wave, and now you must go up the stairs to get to Level 55.1.

items.appearanceHtml:
Level 55.1

Level 55.1 is a large room with a massive vertical tunnel in the center, and an elevator is connected to the tunnel that can be used to access the various layers. The tunnel has three areas that you can access: Level 0.2, Level 0.3, and Level 0.11. However, these are smaller versions of their respective levels aside from Level 0.2 and Level 0.3.

Level 0.2

Level 0.2 resembles Level 0 but with white walls and red carpet.

Level 0.3

Level 0.3 resembles Level 0 but with a blue hue present everywhere in the area. The Bacteria roams around Level 0.3.

Level 0.11

Level 0.11 resembles Level 0 but everything is damaged, and the majority of the place is dark. Unlike the original Level 0.11, Level 0.11 has Smilers and a Skin-Stealer roaming around. There are also two groups of 3 inanimate Moai Statues you can find.

• This is an original Sublevel custom-made just for the game.
  • This is the only Sublevel whose parent level isn't in the game.
  • The ambience of this Level is reused from the lights out ambience in The End.
  • The appearance of this Level originates from a Streetview on Google Earth, which appeared as the large tunnel with Level 0 nearby it. The location of it is "Fort Campbell" and it is now removed, however The Hub Mode description mentions how the level first appeared on a satellite image referencing the Google Earth appearance of the level. The idea of the Level containing multiple Sublevels traversable through the use of an Elevator is an original idea.
  • Despite the video where this level was revealed, it doesn't appear on the backrooms wikidot.
  • In the game files, this Level is called "Tunnel Level".
  • The Moai Statues are a reference to the removed Moai Statue Entities in Level 0.11.
  • This Level was originally shown to have a view of Level 0 at the top floor similar to the original image of this Level, but this for some reason got removed.
    • This could have been due to the Level not being connected to Level 0, but instead Level 52.
  • The lever to control the Elevator was originally planned to be separate from the elevator, meaning that you would have to have somebody at the top controlling the elevator, but this would have made the Level impossible to complete in Singleplayer, so the Lever got moved to the elevator for both Singleplayer and Multiplayer. It is unknown why this wasn't kept for Multiplayer, but it might return.
  • If you use a Flashlight to see the dark areas of the top floor, you will find a wall texture similar to the Level 0 one.
  • This is the only Level in the game to have fall damage.

items.name: Level 55.1
items.summary: Level 55.1 Level 55.1 is a large room with a massive vertical tunnel in the center, and an elevator is connected to the tunnel that can be used to access the various layers. The tunnel has three areas that you can access: Level 0.2, Level 0.3, and Level 0.11 . However, these are smaller versions of their respective levels aside from Level 0.2 and Level 0.3.
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Photography left off

Upon spawning in, you might want to pick up some items on the tables of the first hallway, they might be useful. Then, head to the tunnel itself and pick up a Camera from the table next to the 6 computers. On the Camera, you'll see a photo attached to it. To escape the level, you and your teammates must travel to the locations using the elevator and use the Camera to take pictures of the photos.

The Photos Include:

  • If you're playing alone, the first photo is of a message spray painted on one of the walls reading "Who is here?" in Level 0.2. If you're not playing alone, it will be an image of 1-3 players depending on how many people you're playing with.
  • A construction area in Level 0.2.
  • A rundown van in Level 0.3.
  • The Bacteria in Level 0.3.
  • The Moai Statues in Level 0.11.
  • The Skin-Stealer in Level 0.11 (disguised or not).

Part 2: Overgrown

After everything has been photographed, the screen on all the Cameras will turn white and the iron gate in the ceiling will open. Take the elevator up through the gate instead of deeper into the tunnel, and upon reaching the top, a new area will be accessible. In there, there's a room that looks like an overgrown version of Level 0. Most importantly, the room has 2 doors at the end. On the other side of the doors, there is a white void. Jumping in the white void will give you the Wrong Door Ending.

extraSections.html:
• The idea of going from Level 1 of the Backrooms to Level 9223372036854775807 of the Backrooms is pretty ironic.
  • The level number (9223372036854775807) is the maximum 64-bit signed integer limit on a computer.
  • This level has the longest level number, with a total of 19 numbers in the level number.
  • The level number is commonly shortened to just 922 when mentioned in some parts of the game. This has especially happened a lot after Version 1.0.
    • 922 is now the level's new official number on the Backrooms Fandom wiki where it originates due to new rules enacted to fill in the first cluster of 1,001 levels before moving onto the next.
  • This is the only procedurally generated level in the game.
  • The floor that the incorrectly labelled 94 will be at depends on the difficulty. On Easy it's somewhere on floors 10-30. For Normal and Hard, it's somewhere on floors 20-50. And in Nightmare, it will be somewhere on floors 10-the first ∞ floor.
  • If you travel beyond floor 99, every floor after that will just be labelled "∞" and the floors will keep on going forever.
  • Originally, this level was accessed between The End and Level 94 and was apart of the main level path, but in Version 1.0, this was changed. Level 9223372036854775807 is currently the only level to have went from a main path level to a secret level.
    • Because of this, the level used to have a M.E.G. Base Entry, but it was removed for becoming a secret level. The description of it was "Level 9223372036854775807 is an incredibly tall, dark, spiral staircase surrounded by walls, and is currently undetermined in size. nobody has been able to reach the top, so it leads many to believe that it's infinite.", It was listed as "Class 0 - No Danger", its Sanity Danger was "Class 0 - No Sanity Risk", its file name was "Level 92235...", and it would have shown your fastest escape.
  • If you have a save file at Level 9223372036854775807 before Version 1.0, update your game and load the save file, and complete Level 9223372036854775807, you will get The Suburbs Ending like normal. Afterwards, the game will think that you have unlocked both the Wrong Door Ending and The Suburbs Ending in the save file.
  • This level originally had a completely different design, and Fancy decided to redo it to make it look more realistic.
  • On the Backrooms Fandom Wiki where this level originates, it is now known as Level 922.
  • In the original Backrooms lore, jumping from the 5,000th floor would let you escape into reality, however, in the game this is not true.
extraSections.title: Trivia

• Level 974's development started in August of 2022, and released on October 23rd, 2025 in Version 1.0 3 years after starting development, making it the Level that has taken the most time to make.
  • Fancy mentioned in his 11th Devlog how this Level was initially one room. This could have actually not been the case however because behind Mr. Kitty standing in the door in the old appearance, you could see hallways.
  • Initially, the appearance of this Level was resemblant of the Level in the original lore with everything being pink and not rundown, however in order to prevent confusion from regular players who do not know about the Backrooms lore who would be really confused as to why they're hours into a horror game and suddenly enter in a bright pink house with plushies, Fancy decided to redesign the entire Level to have it be a rundown multifloored house instead.

items.name: Level 974
items.summary: Level 974 is an old, 2 story abandoned and rundown pink house. This Level is the home of Mr. Kitty .
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: The Audience

Once you first spawn in, you'll be in the stage room. After a few seconds of being in the level, Mr. Kitty will spawn. He's completely harmless if you're staring at him, but if you look away, he will get closer and closer the longer you look away. Look away for too long and he'll reach you and kill you. In order to escape, you must go around the level and collect 12 Toys and seat them all down in the 12 seats in the stage room.

Part 2: A Dark Truth

Once all 12 Toys are seated, lights will shine down on x's marked on the stage. The number of lights that spawn depends on how many players you are with. To open up the curtains, everyone must stand on each of the lights, and the stage curtains will open up, revealing 4 Wanderers seated down with a projector in the back reading "Sweet dreams little one". When this occurs, a gas will start spraying down from the ceiling as the player falls down and closes their eyes.

What you have to do is in the exit room of Level 188, hide behind the glass panels and wait for the Aranea Membri to get to the other side of the room. If it's far away enough, make a run for the door. Be sure you're as close to the door as possible when you head to it to avoid the Aranea Membri getting to you.

    • This was due to the fact that people wanted it to be an anti-cheat level but Fancy didn't think it made sense to have an entire level if a player decides to cheat. The workaround to add it was to have it as a normal level you can find instead of one you get to by cheating, which makes the name feel a little pointless.
  • Fancy almost talked about this level in his 12th Devlog but was interrupted by Maic.
  • It was revealed in Fancy's 13th Devlog that this level will have a proper objective in which players have to go through keys to find the right one to the door and if they take too long, they will die to whatever is behind the door. items.summary: Overgrowth looks just like Level 0 , however it's very damaged and abandoned. The level has things like beige wallpaper and moist carpet just like Level 0 , graffiti all over the walls, missing ceiling tiles, damaged ceiling tiles, bacteria deposits over the walls and ground, and air with bacteria making it hard to breathe in, which has caused previous Wanderers to pass out.
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Getting the gates open

Once you first spawn in, you'll be in the small built in the level. There are 3 locked gates and they lead to the rest of the level. To unlock it, head to the Death Slides and enter the room on the right of them. In it, there is a glass view of a Bone Thief hole and it can be reached through the Death Slides. To unlock the gates, pull the lever in this room and the gates will open.

Part 2: Death Slide Disarming

In order to escape the level, you must disarm the Death Slides from danger, and in order to do that you have to roam around the level and pick up passed out Wanderers and push them down the Slides. Be careful finding the Wanderers, as a large amount of Bacterias roam around the level. If one spots you, just hide in a hole in the wall, and it will leave. If a Slide isn't disarmed, riding it will lead you to a deadly fan. If a Slide is disarmed, the fan will be out of the way, but you'll end up in front of the Bone Thief hole and die. Find all 4 of the passed out Wanderers and use them to disarm all 4 Death Slides. Once this is done, ride down anyone, and they will instead send you to The Grassrooms.

• The Bunker seems to act as a secret hideout hidden below The M.E.G. Base, considering the Elevator and M.E.G. connections.
  • The main level layout pinboard has a couple mistakes:

  • In a video from the Escape the Backrooms TikTok account, it was said that the bunker room in the level would not get good reviews on TripAdvisor, which would make sense considering the condition of the room.

This appearance was confirmed when a picture of the level was shown by Mattfor on the Discord Server and on the Escape the Backrooms TikTok account.
  • This level might be based on Level 92 from the Backrooms Wikidot since one exit to the level on the Wikidot page calls Level 92 "the Bunker".

items.summary: The Bunker is a small, abandoned hideout bunker that seems to have been made by M.E.G. It's very small and only contains a few rooms, and the M.E.G. base area has a liminal room behind glass.
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Getting into the Bunker

There are multiple ways to enter the level. One is by obtaining an Ending (except for the Final Escape Ending) in which you'll wake up in the bunker room where a hall connects to it and you can use an ID Card to get back to the M.E.G. base area of the level. Another way to get here is by using the Elevator in The Hub's M.E.G. Base which is open if the Wrong Door Ending has been unlocked. Just press the "B" button at the bottom of the floor selection, and you'll be here in no time. The Elevator will bring you to the M.E.G. base area in the level, and in it, you can find 2 pinboards that show the layout of all the levels in the game which also shows where secret levels can be found.

Part 2: Off to Overgrowth

To get to the next level, go to the M.E.G. secured door in the M.E.G. base area and use an ID Card to scan on the card scanner to unlock it. You can access your ID Card by pressing C. The dark hallway on the other side leads to Overgrowth.

items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: The First Rooms

Once you first spawn in, you're going to want to explore around until you find a large room with a nearly entirely grassy ground and 2 levers. Pull these levers once to move the bridge pieces across to the other side. Upon getting on one, it will take you to the other side. On the other side of these bridge pieces is a concrete block with Ropes and a wooden plank being held up by a rope. Pull a lever that's on this concrete block, and it will unlock a metal door from below. Enter this door and inside are some Knives. Pick up one and return to the concrete block. Then, use the Knife to cut the rope on the plank and walk on the plank to the next room. In the next room, go up the staircase, and at the top is another rope you can cut. Cut it and a wood piece drops on glass below, opening the next area.

Part 2: M.E.G. Buttons

In the next area, there is a metal door locked off by 4 red lights. To make the lights green, go through the maze that's right next to the door and press all 4 of the M.E.G. operated buttons. Once all 4 are pressed, the metal door will open. On the other side is a large room with platforming structures. On here, you must find another rope holding up a plank and cut it with a Knife. This will open up the path to the next and final room. In this last room, you must press 4 M.E.G. operated buttons to unlock a metal door once again, but instead of finding the buttons in the maze, you must find a staircase that lets you walk on top of a bit of the maze allowing you to get to a more isolated staircase that leads up to an upper area containing the 4 buttons. Press them all and return to the metal door. It will be opened, and on the other side is the exit Elevator, however upon entering, you'll notice it has a very large number of buttons. As soon as you enter the Elevator, you will get the Empty City Ending.

items.appearanceHtml: Abandoned Outpost is a rundown and abandoned M.E.G. owned laboratory located in Darkened Suburbs. It seems to have been used by M.E.G. to study Wretches. The base contains a bed with a Wretch in it, showing it was being studied at one point in time, along with a lot of spilled laboratory equipment, Lockers, some Vents, and a still functioning projector in the base. The entire base appears to have been abandoned for a very long time.

Abandoned Outpost is a base in Escape the Backrooms. It appears to be a lab where Wretches were studied by M.E.G.
extraSections.title: Production

extraSections.html:
Getting into the base

Completing Abandoned Outpost is a requirement for escaping Darkened Suburbs. To get into the base, in Darkened Suburbs, players must enable three Computers found in the houses of the Level, which opens the gate to the large house with the base inside. The base is located inside the house, and in order to find the base, look for the Door with a spray-painted arrow pointing at it. On the other side is the basement to the house which leads to the base, and once a player (or all the players if in Nightmare) walks into the basement, it will send everyone in the lobby to the base.

Part 1: Figuring out the mission

Upon entry, simply head forward and go through the two hallways that lead into a room with an Emergency Platform and a projector. Once someone in the lobby enters the room, the projector will automatically play a video explaining how to turn a Wretch back to normal. The rest of the base can't be explored until the video is done because the M.E.G. Door leading to the rest of the base remains automatically closed until the video finishes. In the room is a Note on the table with the Filtering Unit that mentions a Wretch in the base that has an Administrator Card that can get one anywhere, hinting at what the mission is.

Part 2: The Concentrate

Once the video ends, the M.E.G. door to the rest of the base will automatically open and remain open forever, allowing exploration of the entire base. At this point, everyone must find 4 bottles of Almond Concentrate and put them into the filtering unit in the projector room. Don't get too comfortable in this base because a Wretch with a Crowbar is roaming it, who carries the needed administrator card. If it spots a player, they can hide in a locker, and it will go away after a short amount of time. Or they can hide inside one of the 2 Vents found in the base but be sure they are opened with a crowbar. Keep in mind that three crowbars can be found in the base, so it's a better idea to hide in a locker, considering there are 16 within the base. Once all 4 bottles of concentrate are collected, make sure they are put into the filtering unit. To put one in, a player can select an Inventory slot that has the concentrate in hand, then press the interact key while looking at the filtering unit. Each red light below the bottles will turn green once each of the almond concentrate are put in place. This will enable the Emergency Safety Shower to function.

Part 3: No more Wretches here

Once the emergency safety shower is ready, everyone will need to get the Wretch in the emergency platform to use it. Just find a way to lure the Wretch to the projector room, and onto the platform standing point. Be sure when luring the Wretch onto the platform that you or another teammate is standing right behind it by the M.E.G. door that leads out of the base. Once it stands on this point, the platform will automatically close trapping the Wretch inside. Now anyone can push the emergency safety shower until it can't be pushed anymore. Turn it on, and the Wretch will be sprayed with the substance from the emergency safety shower that comes from the almond concentrate, which reverts it back into a human Wanderer, making it safe to collect their card. The emergency safety shower also can't be turned on until it's fully pushed. Once the card is collected, the base will no longer be important, and everyone can leave it to finish and escape Darkened Suburbs.

extraSections.html: "Knowing how to deal with emergencies can greatly reduce the risk of an accidental contamination event. Whenever you enter the laboratory, be sure to locate the emergency safety shower, as it may have been moved to a different area. In the event of an accidental contamination, instruct the affected person to stand in the middle of the emergency platform on top of the footprints. The platform should automatically close to ensure everyone's safety. Next, make sure the filtering unit contains 4 full bottles of concentrate. If some of the bottles are only partially filled or missing, place a new bottle in its spot. You'll know that the unit is ready when all 4 lights are green. Now you can push the emergency shower forward until it's locked in place and can't be moved anymore. At this point, you can rotate the handle on the shower to activate it. The platform should remain closed and will automatically open up when the process is complete. Manually opening the platform can cause harm to the affected person or others in the laboratory. Please make sure to clean the equipment and replace all the materials before leaving."

extraSections.title: History

extraSections.html:
The Base

  • Based on the projector video, the canon name of the base seems to be "The Laboratory".
  • Abandoned Outpost is the largest of the 3 bases in the game.
    • Ironically however, it isn't actually that big of a place, especially when compared to other levels.
  • Abandoned Outpost is the only base with an official name mentioned to not have "the" in its name (unless you count the name the Laboratory as its name).
  • Abandoned Outpost is the only base to have an entity within it.
  • Despite Abandoned Outpost feeling like its own level, it acts more as a second part to Level 9.
    • There has been only 2 times where the base has been officially viewed as its own level. The first is in the Part 3 release announcement, where Abandoned Outpost has an image of it as one of the levels added in the update list. The second was in Fancy's 7th Escape the Backrooms Devlog where he called it a level.
    • Because of this, in Level 9 of The Hub Mode, Abandoned Outpost is apart of the level instead of being separate.
  • If a Hub Mode-styled render of Abandoned Outpost existed in the game or elsewhere, it would likely not be the same as the Part 3 release announcement image of the base, as Level 37.2 had a different image of it added as its Hub Mode render instead of the Part 4 release announcement image of the sub-level. The difference being that the Part 4 release announcement image of the sub-level was a still image taken from a non-still scene of the sub-level from Fancy's 10th Escape the Backrooms Devlog, whilst The Hub Mode image of the sub-level came from a still image used as a teaser of the sub-level posted on September 5th, 2023 on the Escape the Backrooms Discord Server.
  • In Fancy's 7th Escape the Backrooms Devlog, he called Abandoned Outpost an abandoned M.E.G. base, which is exactly what he called the M.E.G. Base in his 4th Escape the Backrooms Devlog.
    • An interesting difference is that Abandoned Outpost is noticeably more abandoned than the M.E.G. Base which is still in decent condition.

In-Game Information

  • The base has a lot of parallels to the M.E.G. Base.
    • The M.E.G. Doors can be found in both bases in a similar concept where they are the main Door used in the base.
    • A view of what appears to be Level 0.1 from the glass window is in the base, which in the M.E.G. Base, a view of Level 0 is seen which is much safer than Level 0.1. It also matches how Level 0 is a safe level, so the M.E.G. Base is safe and still in a decent condition while on the other hand, Level 0.1 is not safe so Abandoned Outpost is not safe and in a much rougher condition. Before the base's abandonment, this parallel could be viewed with Level 9 being the not safe location.
      • Wires can be seen on the ground connecting to the base just like in the M.E.G. Base's view of Level 0.
      • The lighting is different from Level 0.1's actual lighting, which would make sense considering that Abandoned Outpost was added before Level 0.1 was. It was only one major update before Level 0.1's addition, but in general it's still 2 different updates.
      • The wall textures are not the same, because Level 0.1 has the same wall textures as Level 0.11. This Level 0.1 has Level 0's normal, undamaged wall textures.
      • If you set your graphics to low, the fog is disabled in the area.
      • There is a missing wall in the area, which can't be seen by normal means, so it probably wasn't important to add.
      • The area doesn't lead anywhere kind of similar to the actual Level 0.1 which just has Noclipping Floors on the ground that are the only way out of it, which is likely because it wasn't designed to be explored or entered since it can't be done in-game. The Noclipping Floors also don't seem to have been something planned during the time.
    • The room with the Wretch on the bed has 4 non-interactable computers in it, just like in the M.E.G. Base.
      • Because of this parallel, this could possibly be hinting at this room being the main room in the base. The M.E.G. Base was likely given 4 Computers since that's the max number of players one can have in a lobby, so if you're playing with 4 players everyone can use a Computer. It could be intentional on Abandoned Outpost's side of it because the Computers can't be used which means there's no player importance to them unlike the Computers in the M.E.G. Base.
  • The base has 2 Vents. One connects to two areas, and the other leads to nothing and is completely pointless aside from using it to hide from the Wretch.
  • The Wretch cannot chase a player into the Level 0.1 area, which can't be reached by normal means, however that's likely the reason for why this occurs.
  • There are 5 possible spawn locations for the Almond Concentrate, which means wherever they spawn, one spawn location will always not be used.
  • Once all 4 bottles of Almond Concentrate are put into the Filtering Unit, it becomes impossible to obtain anymore in the respective save file in which they were put in.
  • If you return to the base after leaving it for the first time after collecting the Administrator Card, the Wanderer will be gone, the Emergency Platform will be fully open, the Emergency Safety Shower will be put back in place (which has barely any logic aside from the Wanderer doing it), and it becomes impossible to die from things in the base since there's no longer a Wretch in the base. It is unknown what happened to the Wanderer.
    • It could also have been the case that the Wanderer is the one who did it, but this doesn't make much sense since they had nowhere else to go expect for the Level 0.1 area behind glass, which for one the glass is in the way and only a few small holes can be used to get to the other side by not breaking the glass and the glass doesn't change when revisiting the base, the actual area in the base doesn't lead anywhere, and the danger of Level 0.1 would make it a strange place to want to go. So, it's likely there wasn't an intended place for them to go.

Details

  • Sanity can't drain in the base, which is weird since it's apart of Level 9 and ironically Level 9 has the highest Sanity drain rate in the entire game.
  • If you shine a Flashlight down the darkness of the first hallway in the base, you'll see that the hallway goes on for a bit longer than what the darkness shows and has a wall that curves.
  • There are 2 "RESTRICTED AREA" messages on the walls. One that's white and is found in the hallway before the projector room, and the other that's yellow and is found in the first hallway after leaving the projector room.
  • A lot of biohazard signs can be found around the base.
  • It's impossible to replicate the footprints on the Emergency Platform perfectly.
  • The Emergency Safety Shower cannot be pushed back once it's pushed all the way to the Emergency Platform.
  • The projector room appears to entirely have a yellow cover covering the walls and ceiling.
  • In the projector room, there are footprints on the ground which seem to be made of a weird yellow material. This material can be seen in the same room on the ground.
    • The footprints don't match the size of the boots of the Wanderer you play as, which likely means they are just there for show.
    • The footprints seem to be a way of hinting that there was once Wanderers here.
  • A yellow warning sign can be seen in the projector room warning people about the rest of the base itself. It says, "WARNING HEAVY CHEMICAL USAGE AREA AUTHORIZED PERSONNEL ONLY NO ENTRY".
    • This isn't an actual problem in the base itself because despite the heavy usage of chemicals in the base, it can't harm you. Logically, this could be because of the hazmat suit players wear.
  • A dead body stored in a yellow cover with a zipper can be found in the projector room.
    • This isn't the first time a dead body can be found in the game, as there are also dead bodies in zone 3 of Level 3.
  • The projector always has a dim light coming through it, even after the projector video is finished.
  • There is no logic behind how the projector automatically plays a video once someone enters the projector room.
  • The projector video at one point shows an image of the base, specifically the room with the Wretch on the bed. Weirdly enough, in the image in the video, the Wretch is not there, the blood below the bed is gone, and the water cooler knocked down nearby the platform is not knocked down.
    • This seems to show the base before it became abandoned.
  • The projector video shows the former Wanderer model, since Abandoned Outpost was added to the game when the former model was still being used.
    • Ironically, in a similar video played in the Level 3999 Elevator, it has the alternate Wanderer model instead, which was the only place the model was seen when the video was added in Part 4. The model shown in that video was likely not made when the projector video was made since the first time it was shown was a blurred teaser of it in Fancy's 7th Escape the Backrooms Devlog, which was uploaded on June 30th, 2023, a bit of time after the release of Part 3 on June 15th, 2023 which added the base.
  • A bunch of audio glitches occur in the projector video. The most interesting is near the end of the projector video when the voice says, "harm to the affected per". When they say these exact words, visual glitches on the video occur as well instead of just in the audio.
  • A few subtitle mistakes can be seen in the projector video.
    • The first subtitles the video shows say the sentence "KNOWING HOW TO DEAL WITH EMERGENCIES CAN GREATLY REDUCE THE RISK OF AN ACCIDENTAL CONTAMINATION EVENT", however every other sentence on the subtitles has punctuation at the end.
    • "Emergency Platform" is said, but the subtitles say "EMERGENCY PLATFORMS". Only one Emergency Platform can be found in the base, however.
    • "close" is said but the subtitles say "CLOSED".
    • "4 full bottles of Concentrate" is said but the subtitles say "4 BOTTLE OF CONCENTRATE".
    • The fourth one is when the sentence "Manually opening the Platform can cause harm to the affected person or others in the laboratory." is said but the subtitles cut it off 3 words early and says, "MANUALLY OPENING THE PLATFORM CAN CAUSE HARM TO THE AFFECTED PERSON OR OTHERS." This could've been due to the space on the screen.
  • Some of the Wretch's footstep sounds don't make much sense with the ground of the base since they are wooden sounds and the ground is entirely concrete.
  • The rough floor seen in majority of the base is a floor design that can also be seen on the Character Select screen and in the Bunker.
  • The state of the ceiling (not in the first hallway or the projector room) in the base looks really similar to the state of the M.E.G. base area in the Bunker and Overgrowth's ceilings (excluding the base area in Overgrowth).
  • 3 exit signs can be found around the base. 2 are red and appear above 2 specific Doors on the hallway side of them, and 1 that's green which is right above the hallway leading to the rest of the base, which might be indicating the hallway leading to the exit.
  • If you have the Wanderer you're playing as stand in or look at while inside any water leaking from the pipes in the base with the game's graphics set to ultra or epic, the game will get a bit laggy.
    • If this is done with high or lower graphics, it will not occur.
  • On the platform that has the bed with the Wretch in it, you can see the numbers 0-1, which could be referring to Level 0.1, which would make sense since there's a view of it in the base.
    • If you shine a Flashlight on the numbers, they will appear yellow.
    • It doesn't make much sense why the numbers would be right next to something not nearby or associated with the view of Level 0.1, so it could likely be referring to something else.
  • The Wretch on the bed has white eyes instead of black ones which every other Wretch has.
  • There is an invisible wall on the platform that has the bed with the Wretch in it, above the bars surrounding the platform, and on the table where an Almond Concentrate can spawn, which seems to be there to keep players from getting past the area where the bed is. It's possible to get to the other side by crouch jumping onto the bed, or in between the space between the bed and the chair.
    • Crouch jumping is a technique where you press CTRL to crouch in the middle of a jump, which allows a player to get on top of a bunch of things they aren't intended to be on.
  • In the farthest chemical storage room from the start of the base, you can shine a Flashlight on the left and right walls to reveal a biohazard sign. Before shining the Flashlight, the biohazard sign can be seen but not very clearly.
  • The Inventory has 3 slots for Items to carry by using the 1 2 and 3 keys but there's 4 bottles of Almond Concentrate to put into the Filtering Unit. This means if you have all 4 bottles of Concentrate, you'll need to put in 1 before you can have all 3 ready in the carrying Inventory slots.
  • The moment the Wretch turns back into a Human is hidden by the gas from the Emergency Safety Shower. Without the gas, you can see that the Wretch model gets instantly replaced by the Wanderer model.
    • This is likely the case because modeling out the Wretch transformation into a Human wasn't necessary since it was just hidden by the gas.
  • When the Wretch is turned back into a Human, only 2 sides of the Emergency Platform open up. This seems to be the case because of the Emergency Safety Shower being in the way of one side, to make sure the Wanderer has something to lie on, and there needs to be a way for players to collect the Administrator Card.
  • The Wretch's Crowbar is completely gone when it's turned back into a Human. There is no explanation on how or why it's gone.
  • If you leave the base after turning the Wretch back into a Human and if the Administrator Card isn't collected, they will turn back into a Wretch once you re-enter the base. This doesn't stop until the Administrator Card is collected.
  • Once the Wretch is turned back into a Human, the Administrator Card can't be collected until the smoke from the Emergency Safety Shower is cleared.

Lore & Theories

  • This seems to be the most Lore significant area in the entire game, considering the heavy importance of Wretches here, an outbreak is mentioned in Level 9's Hub entry to have occurred here, and the projector video mentions some things that don't occur in the base mission and can't occur when in the base.
    • The things it mentions that don't occur in the mission include an accidental contamination event, the Emergency Safety Shower being moved to a different area, manually opening the Emergency Platform, and cleaning the equipment and replacing all the materials before leaving.
  • There might be a reason for Abandoned Outpost being located in Level 9.
    • One possibility is that the Wretches in Level 9 might have already been in the level, which would probably make M.E.G. curious on them and want to make a base in the level to study them.
    • Another possibility is that the Wretches in Level 9 were members of the base who became Wretches from the outbreak mentioned in Level 9's Hub entry. The only evidence to support the Wretches in Level 9 being members of the base is the Crowbars they have. Crowbars cannot be found in Level 9, but 3 Crowbars can be found in Abandoned Outpost. They're likely just there so players have something to break open the 2 Vents found in the base, but for all we know it could have Lore significance.
  • The base is very hidden, which seems quite important considering the house it exists in is surrounded by a metal fence, the gate can only be opened by finding 3 Computers and enabling them which are all scattered in the houses of Level 9, and the idea of putting the base in the largest house in Level 9.
  • There might be Lore importance as for why Sanity doesn't drain in the base. The place seemed to be designed as a safe place just used for studying and if it would remain safe, Sanity would have to be kept from draining. It could also just be a gameplay thing to keep the base from being too hard to complete since the base has Wretch safety features such as the Emergency Platform and Emergency Safety Shower so Sanity might have been something in mind for the past inhabitants of the base, especially since this is a place where they studied Wretches.
  • The Hub entry for Level 9 mentions there being reports of an outbreak in the base. It also mentioned how this cannot be confirmed.
    • In the base, it very much seems like something happened that caused its abandoned appearance, and it could very much have been the outbreak.
  • Fancy implied that running out of Sanity is the cause for becoming a Wretch in his 5th Escape the Backrooms Devlog, which at first it would seem like the Wretch in the base became a Wretch inside the base itself, however that cannot be the case because it's impossible for Sanity to drain in the base. This likely means the Wanderer who turned into a Wretch became a Wretch in a different place, most likely Level 9 since it has the highest Sanity drain rate and Abandoned Outpost is located next to Level 9.

Audio

  • The ambience of the base is stored in a folder in the game files with other similar sounding ambiences that appear in other places in the game. All the ambiences seem to have been created by the same people, because they all sound somewhat similar to each other.
    • The other ambiences that can be found in here include the ambience of the Bunker, Overgrowth, The End lights out event/Level 55.1, Level 10's 2nd ambience/Level 52, floor 2 and 3 of Level 188, and Level 37.2's 2 2nd ambiences.
    • All the ambiences in here follow a style in the file name, which goes "PLSNC_ND_LOW_(nickname)_LOOP_(1 or 2)". The nickname in place for Abandoned Outpost's ambience is "Shades_Unblest", and it uses the number 1 as its loop number.
  • Not only is the audio file of the projector video titled "LabVideo", but the video file of the projector video is titled "LabVideo".

Bugs

Not patched

  • Pushing the Emergency Safety Shower back to its starting position will cause it to bounce up, revealing a metal stand for the container. It is unknown if it's intentional for it to be seen or not.
  • The M.E.G. Door that remains automatically closed until the projector video is done is slightly open before it opens up after the projector video finishes, and it doesn't make a sound when it opens either. This has been the case ever since the base was added in Part 3, but it's likely unintentional.
  • There is an invisible wall on the right side of the area where the projector video plays. The wall doesn't go low enough, so it's possible to crouch underneath it. If you do, the Wretch in the base cannot get you and will stop trying to reach you when you're underneath the wall.
  • If you're crouching on the Emergency Platform bars that are closest to the M.E.G. Door that leads out of the base when they close, you will get trapped inside the Trap with the Wretch, which can't kill players when it's inside the Emergency Platform. If you aren't playing with any other players, you will be completely soft locked, but if you're playing with 2 or more players, another player can use the Emergency Safety Shower which opens the Emergency Platform. If you crouch on the very edge of the trap, you'll end up on the top of the Trap when it's closed and should be able to crouch out, or you'll be pushed out automatically.
    • If you do this while standing, you won't get stuck in the Emergency Platform.
  • When the Wretch gets trapped inside the Emergency Platform, the model of the Crowbar its holding changes to the pre-Version 1.0 model of it.
    • This likely has something to do with the Wretch model itself, and the model of when the Wretch is in the Emergency Platform was probably not changed when Version 1.0 released.

Patched

  • Prior to Patch 1.3.2, the host of a lobby could not see the Wretch after restarting in Abandoned Outpost.
  • Prior to Patch 1.3.2, it was possible for a player to grab the Administrator Card from the Wretch while it's still in the Trap if they were playing the game on a controller.

Production Facts

  • Abandoned Outpost is not apart of the original Backrooms lore and is Escape the Backrooms exclusive, as what Fancy based the outpost off of mentions there being a few bases in Level 9 but none have been recorded for entry at the moment, and it doesn't mention anything about an abandoned M.E.G. base specifically like Fancy says.
    • In Fancy's 7th Escape the Backrooms Devlog when he mentioned the base being apart of Level 9, the text highlighted says "There are a few Groups that live and roam Level 9,".
  • Abandoned Outpost was not mentioned, hinted or teased once before it released, meaning its addition in Level 9 was a complete surprise with the release of Part 3.
  • In Fancy's 7th Escape the Backrooms Devlog, he compared this base to the Hub with the Hub being completely safe while Abandoned Outpost is not.
    • The reason why he mentioned the Hub despite it being a level and not a base might be because of the M.E.G. Base located in the Hub.
  • In Fancy's 7th Escape the Backrooms Devlog, he mentioned not to go AFK (which stands for away from keyboard) in this "level", which is certainly a good idea because the Wretch can easily find a Wanderer after some time.
    • In this part, the Level 188 courtyard from Level 0.11 and Level 188 floor 1 ambience is for some reason playing instead of the normal ambience. It's unknown why this is, but it could be either because this was the original ambience of the base, this was a placeholder, or it was just for the video.
    • In the same part, the Wretch that wanders the base comes up and attacks an AFK Wanderer and when it hits the Wanderer with its Crowbar, the Wanderer goes into the ground, which is not possible in-game so it was likely just done as a funny scene for the video.

items.appearanceHtml: The Castle looks like a large Castle with a yellowish white tone that sits on a Cloud. The Castle has 2 rooms on the inside, which include the Funhouse, and the Miniature Amusement Park.

extraSections.html:

extraSections.title: Levels

extraSections.html:
The Funhouse

The first room in the Castle is the Funhouse. The Funhouse appears to be a room with Pillars and circus themed objects. The Funhouse has many decorations like a cannon, boxes, paintings, bowling pins and much more. There is a large door in this room that looks like a Clown that will block the way to the Miniature Amusement Park. To open the Door, you must solve a Puzzle involving putting Cubes on their corresponding Shape. If you do it right, the Door will open, and it leads to the Miniature Amusement Park.

The Miniature Amusement Park

The Miniature Amusement Park is a massive room with many objects like streetlights, Liquid Pain Dispensers, a Roller Coaster, and The Animated King. If you complete The Animated King's Trial (which requires you to survive for 2 minutes or 100 seconds) you can get on the Roller Coaster and get to Level 6.

items.name: The M.E.G. Base
items.summary: The M.E.G. Base is a small abandoned M.E.G. Base located within The Hub . The Base has some desks with Computers, Storage Shelves, tables and drawers along with an Elevator that can be used to travel to three total places. There are windows with warnings, and on the other side is a view of Level 0 . The Base is located at the end of a tunnel in The Hub .
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Enter the Base

Once you arrive to The Hub, you must enter The M.E.G. Base to unlock the Gate in Level 1. Before you can access it, you need to Solve a Puzzle. There is a Security Terminal next to the entrance, and it contains 3 slots full of a bunch of words. In order to complete it, match the words with the required code, and press the left mouse button to confirm. Do this 3 times and the door will open. When you first enter the Base, the lights will be out. You will need to find the control panel to turn on all the lights, which is on bottom right of the room. Simply flip all of the switches and the lights will turn on.

Part 2: Unlocking a Computer

In the Base, there are 4 Computers that you can access, but they require a password. A Note can be found on a table in the Level, and it contains 3 passwords. Two of them are crossed out, but the correct one isn't, which is itheardyou. Type this into the computer, and it will unlock.

Part 3: Opening the Gate

In the Computer, there is information on all the levels, but you need to have reached the Level in order to read the entry. There is an application in the computer that you can use to open up the gate in Level 1. It will first warn you, but if you proceed, it will say that the Gate is open, but nobody is authorized to perform rescue missions. And then you will be able to access the garage area of the Final Area. The information on all the levels on the Computers will also include your fastest escape after beating the respective Level, unless it has multiple areas.

items.name: The Manilla Room
items.summary: The Manilla Room is a small room with grey walls, a grey carpet, some furniture, a Locker and a vent that leads to the rest of Level 0. The level also contains an entrance to Level 0.11 .
items.walkthroughHtml:
Part 1: Figuring out the Locker Code

Once you first enter, you will notice that there are boarded up boards that lead to Level 0.11. You must get a Chainsaw to break down these boards, and there's a Locker that has a Chainsaw inside of it. In order to unlock it, you must go to the miniature Level 0.2 within the smaller area of Level 0, and you must use the Light Switch to see the shapes on the Locker. Every time you use the Light Switch, the shape will change, and the number that the shape is on is the order you put it into on the Locker.

Part 2: Entering Level 0.11

Once you have figured out the Locker code, go back to The Manila Room and put in the code, and the Locker will open. If you forget or need it for any other reason it's square, circle, arrow. On the other side is a small Level 0.11 room. After staying in this area for a few seconds, a cutscene will play of the player Noclipping through the ground. Then, you will find yourself in Level 0.11.

extraSections.title: History

extraSections.html:
The level

  • Out of all the upcoming levels, this one has the most amount of information about it, having some information revealed about it and a look at two rooms.
    • The other upcoming levels include the Unknown Dark Level as the one with the second most amount of information about it, only having a blurred picture, and Level ??? as the one with the least amount of information about it, because it's not even known for sure what the level is and was only mentioned.
  • In Fancy's 13th Escape the Backrooms devlog, it was said that the level is a class variable danger level, which means the actual danger of the level is uncertain. No level in the game so far has ever been a class variable, however.

Production facts

  • The highlighted wiki text when Fancy explained this level in his 13th Escape the Backrooms devlog reads, "The Afterparty appears to be an abandoned series of party rooms. Popped balloons, deflated balloons, unpopped balloons, and birthday cakes can be found all around the level.". What Fancy said is, "which appears to be an abandoned series of party rooms with balloons and birthday cakes found all around.".
  • In Fancy's 13th Escape the Backrooms devlog, Fancy described the party rooms as "super liminal rooms with tables that kind of remind you of the ones you'd see at a party".

Lore & theories

  • In Fancy's 13th Escape the Backrooms devlog, Fancy said the lore of this level is that "after the Partygoers won the Fun War, they threw the craziest party in Backrooms history" and that "word around the Backrooms is that the Partygoers really know how to have a good time.". Right after this in that same devlog, a montage plays of the Partygoers partying in the Level Fun+ arcade sector, Level 52 and The Afterparty while the song "BACK2BACK" plays.

Level inspiration

  • This level is based on the Backrooms level "The Afterparty".
